dziecieca.geoTitle: Pediatric dentistry in Opole — our approach
dziecieca.geoText: We see young patients in Opole in a calm, unhurried atmosphere — the first visit is often an adaptation visit, during which the child gets used to the practice. We focus on prevention (fissure sealing, fluoridation, hygiene education) and the least invasive treatment possible — in many cases we treat cavities in children with a laser, without a drill and without pain. We care about positive first experiences that pay off as the absence of dental phobia in adulthood. Parents are part of the process — we explain every step. Clinic in the centre of Opole, easy access, care also for families from out of town.
dziecieca.title: Pediatric dentistry in Opole
dziecieca.subtitle: Calm, pain-free and fear-free — building positive associations with the dentist
dziecieca.intro1: A child's first dental visits shape their attitude to treatment for life. At the Mikrostomart clinic in Opole we make sure children feel safe — no rushing, no scaring and no pain. Our goal is a healthy smile for our youngest patients and calm, positive memories of the practice.
dziecieca.intro2: We combine a gentle approach with modern technology: the Fotona laser, which lets us treat many cavities without a drill, and computer-controlled anaesthesia. As a result, procedures are less stressful and the visit becomes a positive experience.
dziecieca.approachTitle: Our approach to the young patient
dziecieca.approachIntro: A child is not a “small adult” — they need a different pace, language and atmosphere. That is why:
dziecieca.approach1Text: The first visit involves no drilling — the child gets to know the office, the chair and the team, settles in and builds trust.
dziecieca.approach2Text: We explain everything calmly and visually, without scary words. The child knows what will happen — and that reduces anxiety.
dziecieca.approach3Text: We take our time. We never force — we build cooperation step by step, at the child's own pace.
dziecieca.approach4Text: The parent is part of the process. Together we set a prevention plan and teach the child how to care for their teeth.
dziecieca.prophylaxisTitle: Prevention — the best treatment is prevention
dziecieca.prophylaxisText: With children we focus above all on prevention: fissure sealing (sealing the molars), fluoride varnish and fluoridation to strengthen enamel, dietary guidance and learning proper brushing technique. Regular preventive visits let us catch cavities very early — when treatment is simplest and least invasive.
dziecieca.painlessText: The erbium laser (Er:YAG) can prepare many cavities without contact — without the unpleasant vibrations and the characteristic drill sound that stress children the most. It is one of the most effective ways to treat the youngest patients comfortably and calmly.
dziecieca.milkTeethTitle: Baby teeth — why they are worth treating
dziecieca.milkTeethText: Baby teeth are not “teeth for a moment”. They play a key role: holding space for permanent teeth and enabling proper chewing and speech development. Untreated decay in baby teeth can cause pain, infection and damage the buds of permanent teeth. That is why we treat them just as carefully as adult teeth.
dziecieca.hygieneTitle: Oral hygiene in the youngest children
dziecieca.hygieneText: It is worth caring for a child's teeth from the moment the first tooth appears. We teach parents and children proper brushing technique, select toothpaste with an age-appropriate fluoride content and show how to build a daily habit. We carry out hygiene appointments for children gently and in a friendly atmosphere.
dziecieca.whyText: We combine patience and empathy with modern equipment. Prevention and hygiene for our youngest patients are led by our hygienist Elżbieta Nowosielska, while treatment — using the laser and computer-controlled anaesthesia — is carried out to be as stress-free as possible. We want your child to like the dentist for years to come.
dziecieca.faqQ1: At what age should I bring my child for the first visit?
dziecieca.faqA1: Ideally around the age of one or once the first teeth appear — for an acclimatisation visit. The earlier, the easier it is to build positive associations and catch any problems early.
dziecieca.faqQ2: Do baby teeth need treating if they will fall out anyway?
dziecieca.faqA2: Yes. Baby teeth hold space for permanent ones, enable chewing and speech development, and untreated decay can damage the buds of permanent teeth and cause pain.
dziecieca.faqQ3: Is laser treatment safe for a child?
dziecieca.faqA3: Yes — the dental laser is proven technology, and it is especially valued in children because it allows many cavities to be treated without a drill, vibration and often without anaesthesia.
dziecieca.faqA4: It is the painless sealing of the grooves (fissures) on the chewing surface of molars with a special resin. It creates a barrier protecting against decay in the most vulnerable spots.
dziecieca.faqQ5: Can a parent be present during the procedure?
dziecieca.faqA5: Yes. A parent's presence usually reassures the child. We decide individually — sometimes a child cooperates better with the parent close by, sometimes when they watch from a distance.
dziecieca.faqQ6: My child is very afraid of the dentist — what should I do?
dziecieca.faqA6: We start with an acclimatisation visit without treatment, at a calm pace. We use desensitising techniques, drill-free treatment and, if needed, inhalation sedation (nitrous oxide). Fear can be overcome.

zachowawcza.geoTitle: Conservative dentistry in Opole — our approach
zachowawcza.geoText: We treat cavities and defects in patients from Opole with an emphasis on preserving the maximum of the tooth's own tissue. We work under magnification, use new-generation adhesive protocols and premium materials (3M Filtek, GC G-aenial composites), and in many cases — drill-free treatment (Er:YAG laser, ICON infiltration). We also replace old, leaking fillings (including amalgam) with aesthetic composite restorations. The goal is a durable, tight, natural-looking restoration that protects the tooth from root canal treatment in the future. Clinic in the centre of Opole, easy access, care also for out-of-town patients.
zachowawcza.title: Conservative dentistry in Opole
zachowawcza.subtitle: Caries treatment and aesthetic fillings — the foundation of a healthy smile
zachowawcza.intro1: Conservative dentistry is the foundation of caring for your teeth — early detection and treatment of caries and the restoration of damaged teeth with aesthetic fillings. This is most often where the journey to a healthy, beautiful smile begins.
zachowawcza.intro2: At the Mikrostomart clinic in Opole we treat precisely — often using magnification and a laser — and aesthetically, matching materials to the colour and structure of your teeth. Our goal is to preserve as much healthy tooth tissue as possible (hence “conservative”).
zachowawcza.cariesText: The earlier we detect caries, the less invasive the treatment. During regular check-ups we catch changes at an early stage and remove only the diseased tissue, sparing the healthy part. We restore the cavity with a tight seal, returning the tooth to full function and a natural appearance. In advanced cases, when caries reaches the pulp, root canal treatment under a microscope may be necessary.
zachowawcza.fillingsText: We use modern composite fillings that match the colour and translucency of a natural tooth — they are practically invisible. The material is bonded adhesively to the tooth tissue, which helps preserve healthy enamel. The result is durable, aesthetic and fully functional — including in the front teeth.
zachowawcza.noDrillText: Some cavities, especially early ones, can be treated without classic drilling. The erbium laser (Er:YAG) prepares the tissue without contact, and the ICON method (resin infiltration) can stop early caries without damaging the enamel. These are comfortable solutions — without vibration and often without anaesthesia.
zachowawcza.amalgamText: Over time, old “silver” amalgam fillings lose their seal, discolour the tooth and can become a source of secondary caries beneath the filling. We replace them with aesthetic, well-sealed composite fillings — safely, with proper isolation of the working field (rubber dam). It is a matter of both aesthetics and the tooth's health.
zachowawcza.nonCariesText: Not every defect is caries. Tooth wear (abrasion — e.g. from over-vigorous brushing), enamel erosion (from dietary acids or reflux) or abfraction (cervical lesions from occlusal overload, often with bruxism) require a different approach. We diagnose the cause, restore the defect and advise how to prevent further damage to your teeth.
zachowawcza.whyText: We carry out conservative treatment precisely — using magnification (microscope / loupes) and a laser, which lets us spare healthy tissue and achieve durable, aesthetic results. We focus on minimal invasiveness and honest diagnostics: we treat what genuinely needs treating, and we always explain why.
zachowawcza.faqA1: Modern treatment is comfortable. We use anaesthesia (including the computer-controlled The Wand), and many early cavities are treated with a laser — without a drill and often without anaesthesia.
zachowawcza.faqA2: No. We match composite fillings to the colour and translucency of your tooth — they are practically invisible, including in the front teeth.
zachowawcza.faqQ3: Is it worth replacing old amalgam fillings?
zachowawcza.faqA3: If they have lost their seal, discolour the tooth or secondary caries appears beneath them — yes. We replace them with aesthetic composites, safely and with good isolation of the working field.
zachowawcza.faqQ4: I have defects near the gums even though I care for my teeth — what is this?
zachowawcza.faqA4: These are often non-carious lesions (abfraction / abrasion / erosion) — from occlusal overload, over-vigorous brushing or acids. They require different treatment than caries; first we establish the cause.
zachowawcza.faqQ5: How long will a composite filling last?
zachowawcza.faqA5: With good hygiene and regular check-ups, modern composites last many years. Longevity depends on the size of the cavity, its location and habits (e.g. bruxism).
zachowawcza.faqQ6: Does every cavity have to be drilled?
zachowawcza.faqA6: No. Early caries can be stopped with the ICON method (without drilling), and many cavities are prepared with a laser. We decide on the method after assessing the specific case.

periodontologia.geoTitle: Gum disease treatment in Opole — our approach
periodontologia.geoText: We treat patients with gum disease from Opole and the surrounding area comprehensively — from precise diagnostics (pocket probing, attachment-loss assessment), through causal treatment (subgingival scaling, curettage), to laser pocket decontamination (Nd:YAG) that stops inflammation. Periodontitis is a chronic disease, so we emphasise the maintenance phase and regular hygiene — this is what determines lasting results and protects teeth from loss. We tailor the treatment plan individually, taking systemic conditions (diabetes, heart disease) and patient habits into account. Clinic in the centre of Opole, easy access, care also for out-of-town patients.
periodontologia.title: Periodontology Opole — gum disease treatment
periodontologia.subtitle: We stop gum disease before it leads to tooth loss
periodontologia.intro1: Periodontitis (gum disease) is an insidious, often painless condition — and one of the most common causes of tooth loss in adults. Bacteria from plaque and tartar gradually destroy the gum and the bone that holds the teeth in place. The sooner we act, the more can be saved.
periodontologia.intro2: At the Mikrostomart clinic in Opole, Poland, we combine classic, proven periodontal treatment with the Fotona LightWalker laser, which lets us thoroughly disinfect diseased gum pockets and support regeneration of the tissue around the tooth — less invasively and with greater comfort.
periodontologia.symptomsTitle: Warning signs — do not ignore them
periodontologia.symptomsIntro: Gum disease does not hurt for a long time, but it gives signals. If you notice any of the following, it is worth getting examined:
periodontologia.symptom1: 🩸 Bleeding gums when brushing or flossing.
periodontologia.symptom2: 😮‍💨 Bad breath or a metallic taste despite good hygiene.
periodontologia.symptom3: 🦷 Exposed tooth necks, teeth that appear „longer”, gum recession.
periodontologia.symptom4: ↔️ Loose teeth, shifting positions or gaps that were not there before.
periodontologia.diagnosticsText: Precise diagnosis is the basis of treatment. We perform probing — measuring the depth of the gum pockets around each tooth with a gentle periodontal probe. A healthy pocket is 1–3 mm; deeper values and bleeding on probing indicate inflammation and loss of attachment. If needed, we add an X-ray/CBCT to assess bone level. Only the full picture allows us to choose an effective treatment plan.
periodontologia.processIntro: Periodontal treatment is a process — structured and controlled at every step:
periodontologia.step1Text: Probing, bone assessment, medical interview (smoking, diabetes, medications). We create an individual plan and discuss it with you.
periodontologia.step2Text: We remove supra- and subgingival plaque and tartar and clean the root surfaces (root planing). This is the foundation — without removing deposits no treatment will work.
periodontologia.step3Text: With the Nd:YAG laser we disinfect the diseased pockets, eliminating the bacteria responsible for the disease and supporting tissue regeneration. The procedure is precise, bloodless and comfortable.
periodontologia.step4Text: Periodontitis is a chronic disease — regular check-ups and hygiene visits (usually every 3–4 months) are key and keep the result stable for years.
periodontologia.laserText: Nd:YAG laser decontamination is one of the most effective ways to remove bacteria from deep pockets — where traditional instruments cannot reach. The laser also has an anti-inflammatory and biostimulating effect, speeding up healing. It is part of our broader laser dentistry offering.
periodontologia.healthTitle: Gums and the health of the whole body
periodontologia.healthText: Chronic gum inflammation does not concern the mouth alone. Research links untreated periodontitis with a higher risk of heart disease, poorer diabetes control and pregnancy complications. Caring for your gums is a real investment in overall health — and periodontal treatment often improves well-being and quality of life.
periodontologia.whyText: We base treatment on accurate diagnostics and a modern laser protocol. Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. (Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry, RWTH Aachen) uses the laser in periodontology and peri-implantitis treatment, among others, and the maintenance phase led by our hygienist ensures a lasting result. Our focus is stopping the disease and saving the patient's own teeth.
periodontologia.faqA1: Periodontitis is a chronic disease — it does not „disappear” completely, but it can be effectively stopped and kept under control. Early treatment and a regular maintenance phase are key.
periodontologia.faqA2: Procedures are performed under anesthesia, and laser decontamination is additionally minimally invasive and bloodless. Most patients tolerate the treatment well.
periodontologia.faqA3: Bleeding is the most common early sign of inflammation (gingivitis). Left untreated it can progress to periodontitis. It is worth having a probing examination before the disease attacks the bone.
periodontologia.faqA4: Often yes — if we act early enough. Once the disease is stopped and conditions are rebuilt, teeth can stabilise; in selected cases we use splinting.
periodontologia.faqQ5: How often are check-up visits needed?
periodontologia.faqA5: In the maintenance phase, usually every 3–4 months. We set the frequency individually, depending on the severity of the disease and your home hygiene.
periodontologia.faqA6: Yes, a great deal. Smoking is one of the strongest risk factors for periodontitis and worsens treatment outcomes. Reducing or quitting smoking significantly increases the chance of success.

allon4.title: All-on-4 and All-on-6 — fixed teeth on implants in Opole
allon4.subtitle: New, fixed full-arch teeth — often in a single day, on 4 or 6 implants
allon4.intro1: All-on-4 and All-on-6 are modern methods of permanently restoring an entire dental arch (upper or lower) — with no removable denture. The whole bridge of teeth rests on just 4 or 6 implants, and the rear implants are placed at an angle to bypass bone loss and the maxillary sinus. In many cases this lets us avoid extensive bone grafting and shortens the overall treatment.
allon4.intro2: At the Mikrostomart clinic in Opole, Poland, we plan treatment digitally (CBCT scan + intraoral scan), and procedures are performed by Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. — an implantologist holding an M.Sc. from RWTH Aachen. We use implants from reputable systems and PRF (platelet-rich fibrin) for faster, predictable healing.
allon4.qualifyIntro: It is a solution for people missing all or most of the teeth in an arch, or whose teeth can no longer be saved. We most often help patients who:
allon4.qualify1: are missing all teeth (edentulism) in the upper or lower jaw,
allon4.qualify2: have extensive tooth loss that cannot reasonably be restored with bridges on natural teeth,
allon4.qualify3: wear a removable denture that shifts, rubs and lowers quality of life,
allon4.qualify4: have teeth destroyed by decay or periodontitis and qualify for a full replacement.
allon4.procedureTitle: New teeth in a single day — how the treatment works
allon4.procedureIntro: Treatment is carried out in stages, but the patient usually receives fixed temporary teeth on the day of surgery. Here is the typical path:
allon4.step1Text: We assess the condition of your mouth, the amount and quality of bone, and your general health. A 3D scan (CBCT) lets us plan the implant positions precisely.
allon4.step2Text: Based on the CBCT and intraoral scan we design the implant layout and the future bridge. We present the plan and discuss your expectations.
allon4.step3Text: In a single procedure we place 4 (All-on-4) or 6 (All-on-6) implants; the rear ones at an angle to bypass bone loss. We use PRF for better healing.
allon4.step4Text: When implant stability allows (immediate loading), we fit a fixed temporary bridge — the patient leaves with new, non-removable teeth.
allon4.step5Text: After healing (usually 3-6 months) we make the final, screw-retained zirconia bridge — durable, aesthetic and wear-resistant.
allon4.compareTitle: All-on-4 vs All-on-6 vs zygomatic implants
allon4.compareIntro: The number and type of implants are chosen individually after bone analysis. We usually consider three options:
allon4.allon4Text: A full arch on 4 implants (2 straight at the front + 2 angled at the back). Works well with moderate bone loss and a more accessible budget. Often without bone grafting.
allon4.allon6Text: A full arch on 6 implants — better distribution of chewing forces and a greater safety margin. Recommended with sufficient bone, bruxism and long-term planning.
allon4.zygomaText: With extreme upper-jaw bone atrophy we anchor implants in the cheekbone (zygoma) — this restores teeth without lengthy bone reconstruction.
allon4.materialsText: We usually make the final restoration as a screw-retained (rather than cemented) full-contour zirconia bridge — a very durable, aesthetic and wear-resistant material. The screw-retained connection makes hygiene and any future servicing easier. Implants come from reputable, well-researched systems.
allon4.immediateTitle: Immediate loading — fixed teeth right away
allon4.immediateText: Immediate loading means we usually fit the fixed temporary bridge within 24-48 hours of placing the implants — with no period of going without teeth. This requires adequate primary stability of the implants, which we assess during surgery. If it is not sufficient, it is sometimes safer to delay loading — the durability of the reconstruction is always the priority.
allon4.authorityDesc: Treatment is led by Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. — an implantologist and microscope endodontist with an M.Sc. from RWTH Aachen (Lasers in Dentistry). We plan digitally, work with reputable implant systems and use PRF (platelet-rich fibrin) for predictable healing. This is a premium approach — precision, comfort and lasting results.
allon4.costTitle: How much do All-on-4 and All-on-6 cost in Opole?
allon4.costText: As a guide, All-on-4 starts at around 30,000-40,000 PLN per arch, and All-on-6 is around 45,000-55,000 PLN. The price depends on the number of implants, the bridge type and any additional procedures. We offer instalments/financing. We provide an exact, binding quote after a consultation and CBCT scan.
allon4.warrantyText: With proper hygiene and regular check-ups, implants last for many years. We provide a warranty on implant treatment, and the screw-retained zirconia bridge is durable and wear-resistant. Professional hygiene and periodic reviews are key to longevity.
allon4.crossText: Missing single teeth rather than a whole arch? See our implantology and prosthodontics (crowns, bridges, implant restorations).
allon4.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about All-on-4 / All-on-6
allon4.faqA1: These are fixed full-arch restorations supported by 4 (All-on-4) or 6 (All-on-6) implants. The rear implants are placed at an angle, bypassing bone loss and the sinus — which often lets us avoid bone grafting. A fixed bridge is mounted on the implants and is not removed by the patient.
allon4.faqQ2: Will I get teeth in a single day?
allon4.faqA2: In most cases, yes. When the primary stability of the implants is sufficient (immediate loading), we fit a temporary bridge within 24-48 hours of surgery — the patient leaves with new, fixed teeth. The final zirconia bridge is made after healing (usually 3-6 months).
allon4.faqQ3: All-on-4 or All-on-6 — which to choose?
allon4.faqA3: We decide after a CBCT scan and bone analysis. All-on-6 (6 implants) gives better force distribution and more reserve — recommended with sufficient bone, bruxism or long-term planning. All-on-4 suits cases with less bone and a more accessible budget. In extreme jaw atrophy we use zygomatic implants.
allon4.faqQ4: How much does All-on-4 cost in Opole?
allon4.faqA4: All-on-4 starts at around 30,000-40,000 PLN per arch, All-on-6 is around 45,000-55,000 PLN — the price depends on the number of implants, the bridge material and any additional procedures. We offer instalments/financing. We give an exact quote after a consultation and CBCT scan.
allon4.faqQ5: How long do All-on-4 implants last?
allon4.faqA5: With proper hygiene and regular check-ups, implants last for many years. We provide a warranty on the implants, and the fixed zirconia bridge is highly durable and wear-resistant. Hygiene appointments and periodic check-ups are key to longevity.
allon4.faqA6: The procedure is performed under anesthesia and we ensure maximum comfort. Temporary swelling and discomfort are possible afterwards, which we manage with medication; PRF supports faster healing. Most patients return to daily activities within a few days.
allon4.faqQ7: Can I travel to Opole for treatment from outside the city?
allon4.faqA7: Yes — we treat many patients from outside Opole and from abroad. We plan treatment to limit the number of visits, and arrange the stages in advance. We help plan your stay and travel.

laser.title: Laser Dentistry
laser.subtitle: Fotona LightWalker laser — less pain, often without the drill or scalpel, faster healing
laser.intro1: A dental laser is not a gadget — it is a precise tool that, in many procedures, replaces the drill and the scalpel. At the Mikrostomart clinic in Opole, Poland, we work with the Fotona LightWalker, one of the most advanced dental laser systems in the world. With it, many procedures — from root canal treatment, through gum surgery, to periodontal pocket disinfection — are performed more accurately, less invasively and with greater comfort for the patient.
laser.intro2: Laser therapy is a field in which we genuinely specialise. Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. completed a Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry at RWTH Aachen University — as the second person in Poland and the youngest in the country — and shares his knowledge of lasers as a lecturer at the international LA&HA academy. These are real qualifications, not a marketing slogan.
laser.fotonaTitle: Fotona LightWalker — two lasers in one device
laser.fotonaIntro: LightWalker combines two wavelengths that complement each other in daily practice. This makes it possible to match the working mode precisely to a given procedure — from delicate soft tissue, through the hard structure of the tooth, to deep canal disinfection.
laser.advantage1Title: Er:YAG 2940 nm — hard tissues and canal irrigation
laser.advantage1Text: The erbium laser precisely prepares enamel, dentin and bone, and in endodontics it activates SWEEPS/PIPS technology — a shockwave that flushes bacteria out of the lateral canals that a traditional file cannot reach.
laser.advantage2Text: The neodymium laser penetrates deep into the tissues: it disinfects canals and gum pockets (eliminating up to 99% of bacteria, including E. faecalis), stops bleeding during procedures and speeds up healing through biostimulation.
laser.advantage3Text: The laser beam works point by point, sparing healthy tissue. The cut is clean and the working field clear. This translates into a less extensive procedure and a better aesthetic result.
laser.advantage4Text: Many laser procedures can be performed with minimal anaesthesia or none at all. The absence of drill vibration and turbine noise is a relief, especially for people with dental anxiety.
laser.applicationsIntro: The laser is not a separate „procedure” — it is a technology that supports treatment across many areas. Here are the most important applications in our clinic:
laser.app1Text: Laser-activated irrigation (SWEEPS) and Nd:YAG disinfection significantly improve the effectiveness of root canal treatment, especially in difficult, infected teeth and in retreatment (Re-Endo).
laser.app2Text: The erbium laser prepares decayed tissue without contact, without unpleasant vibration or turbine noise. It is a comfortable solution, particularly for children and people with dental phobia.
laser.app3Text: Frenectomy (releasing the frenulum), clinical crown lengthening, gum line correction and lesion removal performed with the laser are bloodless, usually require no stitches and heal quickly.
laser.app4Text: Laser decontamination of gum pockets removes the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease and supports regeneration of the tissues around the tooth.
laser.app5Text: Inflammation around an implant can lead to its loss. Laser decontamination of the implant surface is one of the most effective ways to stop this process.
laser.app6Text: The low-level laser accelerates wound healing, soothes aphthous ulcers and cold sores, reduces tooth sensitivity and supports recovery after surgical procedures.
laser.app7Text: The laser supports aesthetic treatments — from preparing the tissues for veneers, through laser-assisted whitening, to shaping the gum line for a harmonious smile.
laser.authorityTitle: The laser in expert hands — M.Sc. RWTH Aachen
laser.authorityDesc: In laser dentistry, what matters is not the device itself but the knowledge and experience of the operator. Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. is a graduate of the prestigious Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry programme at RWTH Aachen University (with distinction) — as the second person in Poland and the youngest in the country. He is a lecturer at the Laser and Health Academy (LA&HA), with which he has presented at symposia in Slovenia (2019, 2023) and as a keynote speaker in Poland (2022). As a result, the laser protocols used at Mikrostomart are based on current scientific knowledge, not intuition.
laser.benefitsIntro: Laser treatment brings measurable benefits, not just a technological curiosity:
laser.benefit1: Less pain during and after the procedure — often without anaesthesia.
laser.benefit2: No bleeding during soft-tissue procedures and usually no stitches.
laser.benefit3: Faster and calmer healing thanks to biostimulation.
laser.benefit4: Higher disinfection effectiveness — a lower risk of recurring infection.
laser.benefit5: Minimal invasiveness — we preserve healthy tissue.
laser.benefit6: Greater comfort and less stress, especially for people afraid of the dentist.
laser.crossTitle: The laser in root canal treatment
laser.crossText: The laser shows its greatest benefits in endodontics — laser-activated irrigation and disinfection make it possible to save teeth that would previously have been condemned to extraction. See what microscope-assisted, laser-supported root canal treatment looks like.
laser.crossCta: See root canal treatment under the microscope
laser.costTitle: How much does laser treatment cost?
laser.costText: The laser is a supporting technology within a specific procedure (e.g. root canal treatment, gum surgery or periodontal treatment), so there is no single, fixed price „for the laser”. The final cost depends on the type and scope of treatment — we always determine it individually after a consultation and examination.
laser.faqA1: It is usually less painful than conventional methods. Many laser procedures can be performed with minimal anaesthesia or none at all, and the absence of drill vibration adds to the comfort.
laser.faqA2: Yes. The dental laser is a proven, certified technology that has been used in dentistry for years. Safety depends above all on the operator's knowledge — here, procedures are led by a qualified laser therapy specialist (M.Sc. RWTH Aachen).
laser.faqQ3: Can the laser treat cavities without the drill?
laser.faqA3: In many cases, yes — the erbium laser prepares decayed tissue without contact, without unpleasant vibration or turbine noise. We decide whether this method can be used after assessing the specific cavity.
laser.faqQ4: How does laser root canal treatment work?
laser.faqA4: After shaping the canals, we activate the irrigating fluids with the laser (SWEEPS technology), which creates a shockwave that flushes bacteria out of the lateral canals. We then disinfect the canals with the Nd:YAG laser, eliminating up to 99% of microorganisms.
laser.faqQ5: Does a laser procedure heal faster?
laser.faqA5: Yes. The laser cut is bloodless and precise, and the biostimulation effect speeds up tissue regeneration. After gum procedures we usually place no stitches, and post-operative discomfort is reduced.
laser.faqQ6: Can the laser help save my implant with inflammation (peri-implantitis)?
laser.faqA6: In many cases, yes. Laser decontamination of the implant surface is one of the most effective methods of stopping peri-implant inflammation. It is crucial to come in early enough — book a consultation so we can assess the situation.
laser.faqQ7: Do you treat children and people with dental anxiety using the laser?
laser.faqA7: Yes — this is one of the laser's main advantages. The absence of the drill, vibration and often anaesthesia makes the procedure calmer and less stressful, which matters enormously for children and patients with dental phobia.

oferta.title: Our Services

metamorfozy.title: Smile Makeovers in Opole
metamorfozy.description: See how we transform our patients' smiles and lives in Opole, Poland — veneers, bonding, crowns, implants and All-on-X. Every makeover tells a different story.
metamorfozy.introText1: A smile makeover is a comprehensive, planned treatment that combines aesthetics with function — from correcting the colour and shape of teeth to a full bite reconstruction on implants. At Mikrostomart in Opole, Poland, we design every makeover individually, to suit the patient's face, expectations and possibilities.
metamorfozy.introText2: The result is no accident — we plan it digitally (Digital Smile Design), and the work is carried out by a team experienced in aesthetic dentistry, prosthodontics and implantology (Dentist Marcin Nowosielski, M.Sc. RWTH Aachen).
metamorfozy.pathsIntro: A makeover can be achieved with different methods — the choice depends on the condition of the teeth, expectations and budget. We most often use:
metamorfozy.path_veneers_desc: Thin shells on the front teeth — changing colour, shape and alignment. A spectacular result with minimal intervention.
metamorfozy.path_bonding_desc: Shaping teeth with composite resin, often without grinding — a quick correction of shape, defects and gaps.
metamorfozy.path_crowns_desc: E.max and zirconia crowns and bridges — rebuilding heavily damaged teeth with full aesthetic and functional restoration.
metamorfozy.path_implants_desc: Restoring single missing teeth on implants — a natural look and full chewing function without touching neighbouring teeth.
metamorfozy.path_allonx_desc: Full, fixed reconstruction of an entire arch on implants — a new smile for edentulism or extensive loss, often in a single day.
metamorfozy.dsdText: Before we start treatment, we design the new smile digitally based on photos and an intraoral scan. With Digital Smile Design you see the expected result before the procedure, and we precisely plan every stage — the shape, proportions and colour of the teeth matched to your face.
metamorfozy.costText: The cost and time of a makeover depend on the chosen method — from single visits for bonding or whitening to several months of treatment for implant-supported reconstruction. We always determine the scope and quote after a consultation and diagnostics. Eligibility depends on the condition of teeth, gums and bone — we assess it during the visit and propose a plan tailored to your expectations.
metamorfozy.faqHeading: Frequently asked questions about smile makeovers
metamorfozy.faqQ1: How do I start a smile makeover?
metamorfozy.faqA1: With a consultation. We assess the condition of teeth, gums and bite, discuss your expectations and — if needed — perform diagnostics (photos, scan, CBCT if required). Based on this we design a treatment plan and present the possible paths and a quote.
metamorfozy.faqQ2: How long does a smile makeover take?
metamorfozy.faqA2: It depends on the method. Bonding or whitening is often 1-2 visits, veneers — a few weeks, and a full implant reconstruction (All-on-4/6) — a few months. We set the exact schedule after a consultation.
metamorfozy.faqA3: Yes — we design the smile to suit your face (Digital Smile Design), choosing the proportions, shape and colour of the teeth so the result is harmonious and natural, not artificial.
metamorfozy.faqQ4: How much does a smile makeover cost?
metamorfozy.faqA4: The price depends on the scope and method — from relatively inexpensive bonding, through veneers, to a comprehensive implant reconstruction. We give a binding quote after a consultation and diagnostics. See also our pricing section.
metamorfozy.faqQ5: Can I see the result before treatment?
metamorfozy.faqA5: Yes — thanks to Digital Smile Design we show the expected result before treatment begins, which lets us refine the details together.
metamorfozy.ctaText: Book a consultation in Opole — we'll assess your case, show the possible paths and design a new smile tailored to you.

oNas.title: Family Clinic
oNas.missionText1: We are a team of professionals running a family dental clinic in Opole. Our mission is to ensure your smile is not only beautiful but above all healthy.
oNas.missionText2: At {brandName}, we combine years of experience with the latest achievements in medical technology. We specialise in microscope dentistry, which allows us to achieve unparalleled treatment precision.

oNas.elaText1: A dental hygienist with passion and a clinic manager at the same time. Thanks to her dedication, the organisation of the clinic is second to none. Patients — both the youngest and the not-so-young — love her cheerful approach to life and work.
oNas.elaText2: With her empathy, she can calm and convince even the most stressed patients to undergo treatment. She takes care of patients' well-being by creating a friendly atmosphere and a sense of security.

oNas.marcinText1: Graduate of the Medical Academy in Wrocław. A man with a passion and calling for dentistry, for whom every day is a new challenge. Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry (RWTH Aachen University).
oNas.marcinText2: Dentist, enthusiast of microscopic endodontics, prosthetic treatment under magnification, dental implantology and laser dentistry.
oNas.marcinText3: He gained professional experience in the best clinics in the region. In 2021, he obtained with distinction — as the second in Poland and the youngest dentist in the country — the title 'Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry'.
oNas.marcinText4: Privately, the husband of Elżbieta and father of little Michael and Lily. Off-road snowboarding fan and bass guitar sound enthusiast.

implantologia.title: Dental Implants Opole
implantologia.subtitle: Teeth implants Opole
implantologia.intro1: Implant procedures at {brandName} are ALWAYS performed using DIGITAL PLANNING, based on which a surgical guide is created for every procedure. This makes the procedure fast, safe, and the implant position exactly as planned in the visualisation.
implantologia.intro2: Such precision cannot be achieved manually. We offer a minimally invasive procedure for placing dental implants using custom surgical guides designed from CT scans.
implantologia.whenIntro: Missing teeth are a serious problem. At {brandName} in Opole we use the latest medical advances:
implantologia.benefit1: We know the exact implant position before surgery (minimal complication risk).
implantologia.benefit2: Implant position is dictated by the future crown (aesthetics and function).
implantologia.benefit3: Shorter procedure time and less invasiveness (often no sutures).
implantologia.benefit5: AnyTimeLoading option (crown loading at any time).
implantologia.structureText: A modern implant consists of a fixture (titanium screw in bone), an abutment, and a crown (the visible part). Each component is individually selected to ensure durability and perfect aesthetics indistinguishable from natural teeth.
implantologia.faqA1: Yes, we precede it with a detailed interview and CT scans.
implantologia.faqA2: No, it is performed under effective local anaesthesia. Post-procedure discomfort is comparable to a tooth extraction.
implantologia.faqA3: Properly placed implants with good hygiene can last a lifetime.
implantologia.faqA4: Uncontrolled diabetes, pregnancy, severe systemic diseases, age under 16.
implantologia.crossLinkTitle: The implant is the foundation — finish it with prosthetics
implantologia.crossLinkDesc: The implant itself is the root anchored in bone. A full tooth restoration requires a crown or a prosthetic bridge. See our full range of prosthetic solutions.
implantologia.benefit7: The procedure is minimally bleeding — which translates to greater psychological comfort for the patient and faster healing.
implantologia.benefit8: An implant with a full ceramic crown is indistinguishable from a natural tooth — in terms of aesthetics, function, and sensation.
implantologia.workflowTitle: How the procedure looks step by step
implantologia.workflowIntro: Implantation at {brandName} is a process, not just a visit. It consists of several stages, most of which the patient undergoes once, and their effects last for many years.
implantologia.workflowStep1Desc: Medical history, assessment of occlusal conditions, clinical analysis of the gums and bone. We perform cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) — it's like a 3D X-ray that shows the exact bone structure in three dimensions and the precise location of nerves and maxillary sinuses.
implantologia.workflowStep2Desc: Based on the CBCT and intraoral scan, we plan in software (3Shape Implant Studio, coDiagnostiX) the exact position of each implant — depth, angle of inclination, distance from nerves and sinuses. The patient receives a visualization and knows exactly what to expect.
implantologia.workflowStep3Desc: We print a personalized implant template that fits precisely on the patient's teeth and guides the drill according to the planned trajectory. It's a 'dental GPS' — the implant goes exactly where it was digitally designed.
implantologia.workflowStep4Desc: The procedure itself, guided by the template, usually lasts 30-45 minutes per implant. In most cases, without cutting the gum and without stitches (flapless). The patient returns home on their own, usually with a mild painkiller.
implantologia.workflowStep5Desc: Soft tissues heal in 2-7 days. The osseointegration itself (the process in which bone fuses with the titanium surface of the implant) takes 2-4 months in the mandible, 3-6 months in the maxilla. The patient has no special restrictions during this time.
implantologia.workflowStep6Desc: After osseointegration, we perform a digital scan, based on which the lab designs and mills the full ceramic crown. Once screwed or cemented — the implant with the crown functions like a natural tooth.
implantologia.complexityIntro: Implantation does not always mean the same thing. We distinguish three levels of complexity that require different plans and treatment times.
implantologia.complexity1Desc: The most common case. 1 implant, 1 crown. Classic treatment time is 3-4 months. Temporary restoration may be possible immediately in cases with good initial stabilization.
implantologia.complexity2Title: Multiple tooth loss — bridge on implants
implantologia.complexity2Desc: Several missing teeth in one segment. 2-3 implants supporting a prosthetic bridge. This avoids grinding healthy adjacent teeth. Treatment time is similar to that of a single loss.
implantologia.complexity3Desc: Patient without teeth or with teeth that cannot be saved. 4-6 implants per arch with immediate loading of a temporary bridge (usually within 24 hours). Possibility to leave the office 'with new teeth' the same day. This is a procedure we have trained intensively — it requires experience, but the result changes the patient's life.
implantologia.postOpTitle: What happens after the procedure — schedule
implantologia.postOpIntro: Most patients are mainly concerned about what is not visible. Below is a realistic schedule of what happens after the implant is placed.
implantologia.postOpD0Desc: Mild swelling and tenderness in the area of the procedure. Usually, 1-2 painkillers are sufficient. Antibiotic prophylactically for 5-7 days. You can eat after 2 hours (on the non-operated side).
implantologia.postOpD7Desc: Soft tissues heal — swelling and tenderness subside on days 3-5. Follow-up visit after 7-10 days (if there were stitches — they will be removed).
implantologia.postOpD90Desc: Osseointegration — the bone fuses with the titanium surface. The patient does not feel this process, but it is the most critical stage. During this time, no loads on the implant (unless a temporary restoration was planned).
implantologia.postOpM4Desc: After confirmed osseointegration — digital scan and crown design. The lab takes 2-3 weeks to complete.
implantologia.postOpM6Desc: Implant with crown ready. Check-up every 6 months along with hygiene — the implant requires special peri-implant hygiene, which we demonstrate to the patient.
implantologia.trainingDesc: Implantology is a discipline where a mistake costs the patient a lot — sometimes for years. Therefore, we do not shorten any stage. Marcin Nowosielski is a graduate of the Oral Surgery Academy (surgery and implantology), a member of PTSL, and holds a Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry from RWTH Aachen — this is significant because in selected cases (bone splitting, regeneration of peri-implant gum) we use laser support, which accelerates healing and reduces complications. We work with implant systems that have a solid position in the market (>20 years of clinical documentation) — because an implant is a long-term investment, and there is no room for experiments with unknown brands.
implantologia.faqQ5: Implant or bridge on adjacent teeth — which is better?
implantologia.faqA5: In most cases, an implant. A bridge requires grinding down two healthy adjacent teeth (loss of healthy tissue) and does not prevent bone loss in the place of the missing tooth. An implant preserves the bone and does not burden the neighbors. A bridge remains a reasonable option where adjacent teeth require crowns anyway, or when implantation is impossible.
implantologia.faqQ6: How long does full treatment take — from the visit to the finished tooth?
implantologia.faqA6: Standard schedule: 1-2 weeks for diagnostics, procedure, 3-4 months for osseointegration (mandible) / 4-6 months (maxilla), 2-3 weeks for crown fabrication. In total, 4-7 months from the first visit to the finished, full ceramic crown on the implant. In special cases (immediate loading technique), this time can be shortened to a few days.
implantologia.faqQ7: What if I lack bone under the implant?
implantologia.faqA7: There are several options. Minor bone loss — we supplement it during the same procedure with bone substitute material (human, bovine bone, or synthetic hydroxyapatite). Greater loss in the lateral segment of the upper jaw — we perform a sinus lift (raising the floor of the maxillary sinus) as a separate procedure 4-6 months before implantation. In extreme cases — bone autograft from another area or zygomatic implants. CBCT shows which path to take before we make a decision.
implantologia.faqQ8: Does the implant require special hygiene?
implantologia.faqA8: Yes. An implant does not require anything special, but it requires consistency. Daily brushing twice a day, flossing or interdental brushes, regular hygiene in the office every 6 months — this is the standard that a patient with an implant cannot neglect. The most common cause of implant loss after years is peri-implantitis (inflammation of the peri-implant tissues), which develops only in patients with neglected hygiene. At the first follow-up visit, we show exactly how to clean.
implantologia.faqA9: Better not. Smoking narrows blood vessels and disrupts healing — in smokers, the risk of implant failure is 2-3 times higher. We recommend at least 2 weeks without a cigarette before and after the procedure. If quitting is not possible — at least significantly reduce during the osseointegration period. This is one of the few things that the patient has a real impact on during treatment.

catKonsultacja.title: Consultation and Diagnosis
catKonsultacja.desc: First visit, treatment plan, X-ray/CBCT diagnostics, second opinion. The starting point for any therapy — we collect history, take photos, and present treatment options.

catZachowawcza.title: Conservative Dentistry
catZachowawcza.desc: Composite fillings, aesthetic bonding, treatment of caries under a microscope. Premium materials (3M Filtek, GC G-aenial) and next-generation adhesive protocols.

catEndodoncja.title: Microscopic Endodontics
catEndodoncja.desc: Root canal treatment under ZEISS Extaro microscope, PIPS/SWEEPS lasers, re-endodontics for difficult cases. Specialty of Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. — PTE + ESE Curriculum.

catImplantologia.title: Digital Implantology
catImplantologia.desc: Implant + ceramic crown under the guidance of a surgical template (guided surgery). All-on-4, bone regeneration, PRF. RWTH Aachen + Oral Surgery Academy.

catAllon4.title: All-on-4 / All-on-6
catAllon4.desc: Fixed full-arch restoration on 4 or 6 implants — new teeth often in a single day. Screw-retained zirconia bridge, immediate loading. Implantologist M.Sc. RWTH Aachen.

catEstetyczna.title: Aesthetic Dentistry
catEstetyczna.desc: Porcelain veneers (E.max), in-office and at-home whitening, composite bonding, Digital Smile Design. Natural effect — not a forced 'Hollywood Smile'.

catOrtodoncja.title: Clear Correct Orthodontics
catOrtodoncja.desc: Invisible clear aligners (Clear Correct), correction of malocclusions from mild to moderately advanced, 3D simulation of the final effect before starting.

catHigienizacja.title: Hygiene and Prevention
catHigienizacja.desc: Professional hygiene (scaling + EMS Airflow sandblasting + polishing), fluoridation, sealing, home hygiene education. Conducted by dental hygienist Elżbieta Nowosielska.

catChirurgia.title: Dental Surgery
catChirurgia.desc: Extractions (including wisdom teeth under CBCT), microsurgical apicoectomies, laser frenectomies with Er:YAG, PRF, ozone therapy. Nitrous oxide sedation available.

cennik.chatIntro: The AI assistant knows our 2026 price list and clinical protocols. Describe your situation, and you will receive a personalized quote with material options. This does not replace a consultation but helps plan your budget.
cennik.faqIntro: Answers to questions about payments, installments, deposits, and what affects the final price of the treatment.
cennik.faqQ1: How much does the first visit cost and what can I expect?
cennik.faqA1: The first visit is a diagnostic consultation starting from 250 PLN — it includes a medical history interview, clinical examination, X-ray (panoramic or point), and presentation of a treatment plan with price ranges. If CBCT is required (e.g., before an implant), it costs an additional 400-500 PLN. After the first visit, you will receive a written plan with specific items and dates.
cennik.faqQ2: Do you offer installments or deferred payments?
cennik.faqA2: Yes — larger treatments (implant, orthodontics, full mouth rehabilitation) can be spread over 0% installments through Mediraty (up to 12 months) or individually arranged internal installments with us (up to 6 months without additional costs for regular patients). Details are discussed after the first visit along with the treatment plan.
cennik.faqQ3: When is a deposit required and how much is it?
cennik.faqA3: A deposit of 200 PLN is collected when booking an appointment lasting over 60 minutes (long root canal treatment, implant, surgery) — we secure the reserved time of the doctor. The deposit is fully credited towards the treatment or refunded in case of cancellation with 48 hours' notice.
cennik.faqQ4: What affects the final price of the treatment?
cennik.faqA4: Three main factors: (1) scope of work — e.g., single-surface filling vs composite reconstruction of half a tooth, (2) material — e.g., metal-ceramic crown vs E.max vs translucent zirconia, (3) clinical difficulty — e.g., re-endodontics of tooth 7 with 4 canals is more time-consuming than endo of tooth 11. Therefore, instead of a rigid price list, we provide ranges, and the exact price after examination.
cennik.faqQ5: Is the online quote (via AI assistant) binding?
cennik.faqA5: No — it is an approximate quote based on typical cases. A binding quote is created after a clinical examination and X-ray, as we often see things not visible to the patient (e.g., hidden caries, microfractures, periapical changes). The AI assistant helps plan the budget and prepare for the consultation.
cennik.faqA6: Yes — all treatments include local anesthesia (most often the The Wand system — computer-dosed, almost imperceptible). Additional sedation options (nitrous oxide 'laughing gas' 150 PLN, pharmacological premedication 80 PLN) are priced separately if the patient needs or requests them.
cennik.faqQ7: Do you issue invoices for insurance reimbursement?
cennik.faqA7: Yes — we issue named invoices with the doctor's PWZ number, ICD-10 code, and procedure description in accordance with insurer requirements (Allianz Care, Medicover, LUX MED, private foreign policies). For patients from Germany — invoices compliant with BEMA/GOZ (for reimbursement from health insurance up to Polish rates).
cennik.faqQ8: Can I get an estimated quote before the first visit?
cennik.faqA8: Yes — through the AI assistant below (available 24/7) or by phone during reception hours. For quotes requiring analysis of images (X-rays from the previous dentist, intraoral images), please contact us via the form at /contact — we respond within 24 working hours.
cennik.faqQ9: How much do All-on-4 / All-on-6 cost in Opole?
cennik.faqA9: All-on-4 starts at around 30,000-40,000 PLN per arch, All-on-6 is around 45,000-55,000 PLN. The price depends on the number of implants and the bridge material. We offer instalments/financing. We give an exact, binding quote after a consultation and CBCT scan.

faq.cat0q0: Why is regular dental hygiene important?
faq.cat0a0: Professional cleaning (scaling, air polishing) removes tartar and deposits that cannot be removed at home. It prevents gum diseases (periodontitis), cavities, and bad breath. We recommend a visit every 6 months.
faq.cat0q1: What is the difference between scaling and air polishing?
faq.cat0a1: Scaling removes hard tartar using ultrasound. Air polishing removes soft deposits and stains (e.g. from coffee, tea) using a pressurised stream of powder. These treatments are usually performed together.
faq.cat0a2: Yes, in-office fluoridation is completely safe and a key element in strengthening enamel. We use certified preparations at appropriate concentrations that protect teeth from cavities.
faq.cat0q3: Which toothbrush do you recommend — sonic or electric?
faq.cat0a3: We strongly recommend sonic toothbrushes. They are gentler on gums while more effectively removing plaque thanks to cavitation (microbubbles), which reaches hard-to-access areas.
faq.cat0q4: My gums bleed when brushing. What should I do?
faq.cat0a4: Bleeding is usually the first sign of inflammation (gingivitis) caused by tartar buildup. Don't stop brushing! Book a professional cleaning — removing the cause usually resolves the problem immediately.
faq.cat1a0: Modern root canal treatment is completely painless. We use computer-controlled anaesthesia (The Wand/SleeperOne), which eliminates pain from the injection stage. Patients often fall asleep during the procedure!
faq.cat1q1: Why is treatment under a microscope better?
faq.cat1a1: The microscope (up to 25x magnification) allows the dentist to find all canals (even additional ones), clean and fill them precisely. This dramatically increases the chance of saving a tooth that might otherwise be extracted.
faq.cat1q2: How many visits does a root canal take?
faq.cat1a2: At {brandName}, we aim to complete root canal treatment in one visit. Thanks to microscope work and advanced machine tools, we can perform the entire procedure precisely and efficiently in 90-120 minutes.
faq.cat1q3: My tooth doesn't hurt — do I need a root canal?
faq.cat1a3: Pain is not the only indicator. Often, inflammation develops chronically in the bone (granuloma) and is only visible on X-ray. This condition is a 'ticking time bomb' and a source of infection for the entire body, so treatment is necessary.
faq.cat1a4: This is a corrective procedure when the original treatment (often years old) proves unsuccessful. It involves removing the old material, finding missed canals, and filling them again under sterile conditions. This is a more complex procedure in which our clinic specialises.
faq.cat2a0: Veneers are thin shells (porcelain or composite) bonded to the front of a tooth. They correct shape, colour, and minor irregularities. They can achieve a 'Hollywood Smile' effect without braces if the issue is minor.
faq.cat2a1: No, professional whitening performed in-office under the hygienist's supervision is safe. We use modern preparations that do not demineralise enamel — they only oxidise the stains within its structure.
faq.cat2q2: How long does the whitening effect last?
faq.cat2a2: The effect typically lasts 1 to 3 years. It depends mainly on your diet (coffee, wine, turmeric) and habits (smoking). Regular hygiene appointments help maintain the results.
faq.cat2a3: Bonding is a non-invasive method of improving tooth aesthetics using composite resin. We do it in one visit, without grinding teeth. Ideal for closing a diastema (gap between teeth) or repairing a chipped edge.
faq.cat2q4: How long does it take to get porcelain veneers?
faq.cat2a4: The process usually takes 2-3 visits. The first is a consultation and design (DSD). The second is minimal preparation and scanning (no impressions!). The third is cementing the finished, beautiful veneers.
faq.cat3a0: Most adult patients can have implants. Contraindications may include certain uncontrolled systemic diseases. The key factor is bone volume — if insufficient, we perform regenerative procedures.
faq.cat3q1: How long does the procedure and healing take?
faq.cat3a1: Implant placement itself usually takes 30-40 minutes and is painless. The osseointegration process (bonding with bone) takes 3 to 6 months, after which the final crown is placed.
faq.cat3a2: Implant success rate is about 98%. Rejection is extremely rare. If it does occur (e.g. due to infection or smoking), the procedure can usually be repeated after healing.
faq.cat3q3: Do implants set off airport metal detectors?
faq.cat3a3: Absolutely not! Implants are made of titanium or zirconia, which are non-magnetic and do not trigger security gates. You can travel worry-free.
faq.cat3a4: It's a procedure where we remove a damaged tooth and place an implant in its place during the same visit. This saves time (one procedure, one anaesthesia) and preserves more bone and gum tissue.
faq.cat4q0: Crown or veneer — which to choose?
faq.cat4a0: A veneer covers only the front of the tooth (aesthetics). A crown covers the entire tooth like a cap (restoration and reinforcement). The decision is made by the dentist based on the degree of tooth damage.
faq.cat4a1: This is a common name for adhesive bridges (fibre-reinforced). They are a temporary or long-term solution that fills a missing tooth gap without heavy grinding of neighbours.
faq.cat4q2: What material do you use for crowns?
faq.cat4a2: We prioritise the highest aesthetics and durability, so we mainly use all-ceramic crowns (E.max) and zirconia crowns. They don't cause gum 'greying' (like old metal-based crowns) and are indistinguishable from natural teeth.
faq.cat4q3: What does the crown-making process look like?
faq.cat4a3: Thanks to our digital scanner, we don't need unpleasant impression moulds. We scan teeth with a 3D camera and send the design to the laboratory. The finished crown is cemented usually within 5-7 working days.
faq.cat5q0: How do Clear Correct aligners work?
faq.cat5a0: It's a set of transparent trays that you change every 1-2 weeks. Each successive aligner gently moves teeth into position. They are removable for eating and cleaning, ensuring hygiene and comfort.
faq.cat5a1: With aligners, pain is minimal — patients describe it more as a 'pressure sensation' for 1-2 days after fitting a new pair. This is incomparably less discomfort than with fixed metal braces.
faq.cat5q2: How long does orthodontic treatment take?
faq.cat5a2: Aligner treatment is usually shorter than traditional. Average duration is 6-18 months, depending on the condition. At the first visit, we'll show you a 3D simulation and tell you exactly how long your treatment will take.
faq.cat5q3: Won't teeth move back after removing braces?
faq.cat5a3: We use retention (a thin wire on the inside or a night aligner) that holds teeth in their new position. This is a standard procedure guaranteeing lasting results.
faq.cat6q0: When should wisdom teeth be removed?
faq.cat6a0: When there's not enough space in the arch (crowding), they cause inflammation, decay in adjacent teeth, or cysts. We assess this based on a panoramic X-ray.
faq.cat6a1: It's platelet-rich fibrin obtained from the patient's blood. It acts as a natural 'super-bandage', accelerating wound healing after extraction by several times.
faq.cat6a2: Don't eat for 2 hours. On the day of the procedure, avoid hot food and physical exertion. Don't rinse your mouth vigorously (to avoid dislodging the clot). Apply cold compresses. If in doubt, call the clinic.
faq.cat6a3: It occurs rarely (about 2-5%), mainly in smokers or women using hormonal contraception. To prevent it, we use PRF and ozone therapy, which dramatically reduce complication risk.
faq.cat7q0: When should a child's first visit be?
faq.cat7a0: We invite you for a first introductory visit at age 2-3, or earlier if something concerns you. It's a playful visit without treatment that builds the young patient's trust in the dentist.
faq.cat7a1: Of course! Healthy baby teeth mean healthy permanent teeth. Untreated cavities in children can lead to bite problems in the future. We use colourful fillings that children love.
faq.cat7a2: Sealant application protects the grooves in molars (permanent sixth teeth) with a special coating. Grooves are where cavities develop fastest because the toothbrush can't reach them. The procedure is quick and completely painless.
faq.cat7q3: My child is afraid of the dentist. How do you handle this?
faq.cat7a3: We have extensive experience with young patients. We use the 'tell-show-do' method. We use 'magic air' (nitrous oxide sedation), cartoons on the ceiling screen, and rewards. We don't treat by force — we build a relationship.
faq.cat8q0: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.cat8a0: We accept cash, debit/credit cards, and BLIK. Instalment payment is also available through MediRaty (prior verification required).
faq.cat8a1: No, we are a private clinic and don't require any referrals. Simply book a consultation appointment.
faq.cat8a2: There is a private, free car park directly in front of the clinic for patients. You'll never have to look for a spot or pay a parking meter.
faq.cat8q3: How should I prepare for my first visit?
faq.cat8a3: If you have a recent panoramic X-ray (no older than 6 months), bring it with you. Prepare a list of medications you take. If a specific tooth hurts, try to identify what triggers it (cold, heat, biting).
faq.cat9q0: Do you offer a treatment warranty?
faq.cat9a0: Yes, we provide a 2-year warranty on fillings and prosthetic work. Maintaining it requires regular check-up and hygiene visits every 6 months (as recommended by your dentist).
faq.cat9q1: What if something hurts after a procedure?
faq.cat9a1: Mild discomfort after certain procedures is normal, but your peace of mind is our top priority. In case of severe pain or concerning symptoms, please contact us — we will always find time to check that healing is progressing properly.
faq.cat9a2: Sterilisation is our absolute priority. We use a top medical-grade autoclave (Class B). All instruments are packed in sterile pouches opened in front of the patient. Dental handpieces are oiled and sterilised after every patient.
faq.cat10q0: Do you have parking at the clinic?
faq.cat10a0: Yes — free parking directly in front of the clinic (entrance from ul. Centralna). No need to search for a spot or pay at a meter.
faq.cat10q1: Are there hotels near the clinic?
faq.cat10a1: Within 3 km of the clinic: Hotel Mercure Opole, Hotel Festival, Hotel Piast, plus several Booking apartments. We can help arrange your stay — just ask at reception when booking.
faq.cat10a2: Yes — patients travel to us from all over Poland as well as from Germany, Czech Republic, Austria and Ukraine. We speak Polish, English and German. Ukrainian translation available on request.
faq.cat10q3: Can I get reimbursement as a German patient?
faq.cat10a3: Polish medical services are VAT-exempt (0% rate), so tax-free does not apply. However, German health insurance (gesetzliche oder private Krankenversicherung) may cover part of cross-border treatment costs — we issue an itemised invoice in German on request. Contact your insurer for the reimbursement procedure.
faq.cat10q4: How do I get to the clinic?
faq.cat10a4: From the A4 motorway: take the Opole-Zachód exit, ~4 km to the clinic (7 min drive). From Opole Główne train station: bus lines 12 or 16 to 'Centralna' stop (~15 min). From the German border (Olszyna crossing): ~200 km, 2-2.5 h drive.

politykaCookies.card3Text: You can change your cookie settings at any time. Restricting cookies may affect some features available on the website.
