leczeniekanalowe.title: Root Canal Treatment {cityShort}
leczeniekanalowe.subtitle: Under the microscope — precision at the highest level
leczeniekanalowe.intro1: At {brandName}, we perform root canal treatment exclusively under an operating microscope with magnification up to 25x. This allows us to see every, even the smallest canal, which could be missed in traditional methods.
leczeniekanalowe.intro2: We use computer-assisted anesthesia (The Wand / SleeperOne), which eliminates pain right at the administration stage. Many of our patients fall asleep during the procedure — the best proof of its painlessness.
leczeniekanalowe.whyMicroscopeTitle: Why does the microscope change everything?
leczeniekanalowe.advantage1Text: We see details invisible to the naked eye — additional canals, cracks, remnants of tissue.
leczeniekanalowe.advantage2Text: Teeth that are doomed to extraction in traditional methods can be saved thanks to the precision of the microscope.
leczeniekanalowe.advantage3Text: We strive to complete root canal treatment in one visit (90-120 minutes), saving your time.
leczeniekanalowe.advantage4Text: We specialize in re-root canal treatment — repairing ineffective treatments from years past.
leczeniekanalowe.processTitle: How does root canal treatment proceed?
leczeniekanalowe.step1Text: Point X-ray + assessment under the microscope. We explain the treatment plan.
leczeniekanalowe.step2Text: The Wand system administers anesthesia slowly and painlessly — no 'pinch'.
leczeniekanalowe.step3Text: Under 25x magnification, we clean and shape the canals using NiTi rotary instruments.
leczeniekanalowe.step4Text: Sealed filling of the canals with hot gutta-percha + restoration of the tooth crown.
leczeniekanalowe.pricingNote: (indicative prices, depending on the number of canals)
leczeniekanalowe.faqA1: Modern root canal treatment is completely painless. We use computer-assisted anesthesia, which eliminates pain right at the administration stage.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ2: Why is treatment under the microscope better?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA2: The microscope (up to 25x magnification) allows us to find all canals, clean them precisely, and fill them. It drastically increases the chances of saving the tooth.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ3: How many visits does root canal treatment take?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA3: We strive to complete the treatment in one visit (90-120 minutes). In complicated cases, 2 visits may be needed.
leczeniekanalowe.faqA4: It is a corrective procedure when the initial treatment has proven ineffective. We remove the old material, find missed canals, and refill them.
leczeniekanalowe.whyMicroscopeIntro: Traditional root canal treatment 'by memory' — where the doctor works with the naked eye, assessing the position of the instrument in the canal based on intuition and control X-rays — is not the standard we strive for. The operating microscope transforms endodontic treatment just as GPS transformed driving in an unfamiliar city.
leczeniekanalowe.laserTitle: What does laser activation PIPS/SWEEPS provide?
leczeniekanalowe.laserIntro: PIPS (Photon-Induced Photoacoustic Streaming) and SWEEPS (Shock Wave Enhanced Emission Photoacoustic Streaming) are protocols for activating irrigating solutions with the Er:YAG laser at 2940 nm. It sounds technical, but the mechanism is elegant — and crucial for the effectiveness of the treatment.
leczeniekanalowe.laserMechanism1: After mechanically preparing the canal with NiTi instruments (VDW Reciproc R50), we introduce an irrigating solution into the canal — 5.25% NaOCl (dissolves organic tissue) and 17% EDTA (dissolves the inorganic layer — the so-called 'smear layer' covering the canal walls after preparation).
leczeniekanalowe.laserMechanism2: We insert the fiber optic tip of the Er:YAG laser into the solution in the tooth chamber. The laser emits light pulses at a wavelength of 2940 nm — this wavelength is highly absorbed by water.
leczeniekanalowe.laserMechanism3: Microbubbles form in the solution, which immediately implode (collapse). Each implosion generates a shock wave that turbulently moves the solution deep into the lateral canals, dentinal tubules, and all the nooks of the canal system.
leczeniekanalowe.laserMechanism4: Classic syringe irrigation reaches the solution about 1 mm from the tip of the needle. PIPS/SWEEPS activation distributes the solution along the entire length of the canal and penetrates into lateral branches — of which there are typically several in one tooth.
leczeniekanalowe.laserNdYagDesc: After activating NaOCl/EDTA, we additionally disinfect the root dentin with the Nd:YAG laser. This wavelength penetrates to a depth of about 1 mm into the dentin and is absorbed by bacterial cells — eliminating reservoirs of infection that no irrigating solution can reach. According to clinical studies (Saydjari 2016), the protocol eliminates 99% of Enterococcus faecalis — the bacteria most commonly responsible for recurrences of periapical inflammation.
leczeniekanalowe.processIntro: Standard root canal treatment at {brandName} lasts 90-150 minutes in a single session. In complicated cases (acute inflammation with an abscess, Re-Endo with a previous crown-root post), we may divide the treatment into 2 visits.
leczeniekanalowe.step5Text: 5-15 ml of 5.25% NaOCl + 5-10 ml of 17% EDTA per canal, activated by the Er:YAG laser in SWEEPS mode (typical parameters: 20 mJ, 15 Hz, conical tip 0.6 mm, 25 µs). This is where the real disinfection occurs — see the section 'What does laser activation provide' above.
leczeniekanalowe.step6Text: After complete disinfection of the canal interiors, we dry them with sterile paper points and fill them tightly. Typically — with warm gutta-percha (vertical condensation technique) with AH+ sealer. In canals with an open apex (e.g., after resorption, in immature teeth) — with MTA (Mineral Trioxide Aggregate), which stimulates the regeneration of periapical bone.
leczeniekanalowe.step7Text: The tooth crown is restored with composite material (SonicFill, GC G-aenial) — depending on the extent of the cavity, sometimes immediately with a full ceramic crown (if the remaining tooth structure is significantly weakened). The visit ends with a control X-ray. Follow-up after 3, 6, and 12 months — as healing of the periapical tissues is a process lasting several months to a year.
leczeniekanalowe.reEndoTitle: Re-Endo — a second approach to a difficult tooth
leczeniekanalowe.reEndoIntro: Retreatment (Re-Endo) is our 'daily bread'. Patients come to us after unsuccessful treatment performed years ago — with pain, an abscess, sometimes after a long history of attempts to save the tooth. Each such case is detective work: we need to remove the prosthetic crown (if present), remove the crown-root post (glass, composite, or metal), bypass the patency of the old filling, find missed canals — all with minimal loss of tooth tissue, as the tooth is usually significantly weakened after many interventions.
leczeniekanalowe.reEndoCaseDesc: Typical case: a patient reports pain and swelling of a tooth treated endodontically 5-10 years ago. Under the microscope, we see that one of the canals was originally missed (e.g., MB2 in the upper first molar), and the old gutta-percha is leaking. CBCT diagnostics confirm periapical changes. Plan: remove the crown, remove the post, locate the missed canal, complete disinfection (NaOCl/EDTA + SWEEPS + Nd:YAG), filling, new full ceramic crown. Time: 2-3 visits spread over several weeks. The long-term effectiveness of Re-Endo is 70-85% in the literature; in practice — when we have a microscope, lasers, and time — it is higher.
leczeniekanalowe.trainingDesc: Endodontics is a specialty that requires continuous education. Marcin Nowosielski is a graduate of the **Endodontic Curriculum of the Polish Endodontic Society** (PTE) — the longest Polish specialist program in endodontics, requiring case presentations and a final exam. Member of the **European Society of Endodontology** (ESE — a European reference organization whose ESE Quality Guidelines set the standard for modern endodontics). Additionally, he holds a Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry from RWTH Aachen — which directly translates to the laser protocols used in our office. Lecturer at the LA&HA Symposium 2019 and 2023 in Slovenia and LA&HA Poland 2022 (keynote 'From Root to Crown — Comprehensive Laser Treatment of Teeth'). In 2024 — lecturer at the 20th anniversary of PTE.
leczeniekanalowe.whenNoTitle: And what if the tooth cannot be saved?
leczeniekanalowe.whenNoText: Honestly — not every tooth can be saved. A root fracture below the bone margin, significant loss of tooth structure without the possibility of prosthetic restoration, long-term acute inflammation with loss of >50% of the bone surrounding the root — these are cases where a planned extraction with implantation is a better option. We do not promise 'saving at all costs', because sometimes the decision for extraction + implant is one that saves the patient 2-3 years of frustration, further procedures, and ultimately the same conclusion. After thorough diagnostics, we state clearly what the real chances are — without false optimism.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ5: Can every tooth be saved with root canal treatment?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA5: No. A root fracture below the bone margin, lack of tooth structure for prosthetic restoration, significant loss of periapical bone — these are cases where we honestly recommend extraction with implantation instead of attempts to save. We state this clearly after thorough diagnostics, because 'saving at all costs' sometimes costs the patient more than the planned extraction.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ6: Does a tooth after root canal treatment require a prosthetic crown?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA6: In the case of molars and premolars — yes, almost always. A tooth treated endodontically is weakened (lack of pulp, decay, or old fillings), and chewing forces are greatest on these teeth. A full ceramic crown or onlay made in our office protects the tooth from fracture. In the case of incisors — not necessarily, sometimes a good composite restoration is sufficient.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ7: How long will a tooth last after root canal treatment?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA7: Properly performed root canal treatment + appropriate prosthetic restoration can allow a tooth to serve for 20-30+ years, often for the patient's lifetime. Key factors are: a tight filling, a prosthetic crown protecting the structure, regular check-ups every 6 months, good hygiene. In the literature, the 5-year success rate of root canal treatment under a microscope is 90-95% — comparable to implants.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ8: Can I eat after root canal treatment?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA8: After standard treatment — yes, but avoid eating hot/cold for 1-2 hours (until the anesthesia wears off) and hard foods on the treated side for 1-3 days (until the permanent restoration is placed). After Re-Endo with surgical elements — a soft diet for 3-5 days and avoiding pressure on the bite.
leczeniekanalowe.faqQ9: What if the tooth still hurts after treatment for several days?
leczeniekanalowe.faqA9: Tenderness to pressure for 1-3 days after the procedure is normal and subsides on its own. Spontaneous or increasing pain > 5 days, swelling, fever — these are signals to call the office. Sometimes additional canal irrigation or a short course of antibiotics is necessary. In practice, ~5-10% of treatments require an additional follow-up visit due to pain response — this is a normal part of endodontics.

estetyczna.title: Cosmetic Dentistry {cityShort}
estetyczna.subtitle: Veneers — Whitening — Bonding
estetyczna.intro1: At {brandName}, we believe that a beautiful smile is an investment in yourself. We offer a full range of aesthetic treatments — from professional whitening, through composite bonding, to premium porcelain veneers.
estetyczna.intro2: Each treatment is planned individually. Thanks to digital smile analysis (DSD — Digital Smile Design), you can see the final result before we start the treatment.
estetyczna.service1Text: Thin porcelain shells bonded to the front of the tooth. They correct shape, color, and imperfections. The Hollywood Smile effect for years.
estetyczna.service2Text: Safe whitening in the office under the supervision of a hygienist. Results up to 8 shades lighter in one visit.
estetyczna.service3Text: Non-invasive aesthetic improvement in one visit. Closing diastemas, repairing chips. No tooth grinding required.
estetyczna.service4Text: Digital smile design. You see the final result on the screen before we start. Full control over the outcome.
estetyczna.faqA1: Veneers are thin porcelain shells bonded to the front surface of the tooth. They correct shape, color, and minor imperfections without braces.
estetyczna.faqA2: No, professional whitening in the office is safe. We use products that oxidize stains without demineralizing enamel.
estetyczna.faqQ3: How long does the whitening effect last?
estetyczna.faqA3: From one year to 3 years, depending on diet and habits. Regular hygiene maintains the effect.
estetyczna.faqA4: Non-invasive aesthetic improvement with composite resin in one visit. Ideal for closing diastemas or repairing chipped edges.
estetyczna.intro3: Patients often fear aesthetics because they associate it with huge Hollywood teeth from advertisements. That's not our style. We design every smile individually — to fit the patient's facial features, character, and age. A good smile is one that no one notices as artificially made — it simply looks healthier and more beautiful.
estetyczna.philosophyText1: We don't create billboards. We create smiles that look like yours — just healthier, more proportional, and better color-matched. The Hollywood Smile (stiff, snow-white, rectangular teeth identical in everyone) is an aesthetic from the 2000-2010 era. Modern aesthetic dentistry is going in a different direction.
estetyczna.philosophyText2: The contemporary ideal is an individual smile — with a slight shading on the edges of the incisors (natural teeth have this), with subtle irregularities, and a color matched to the complexion. Such a smile looks natural and does not reveal that someone has had work done. The patient hears compliments about their fresh appearance, not questions about whether they had something done to their teeth.
estetyczna.philosophyText3: Every plan begins with a portrait photograph + an intraoral scan. We analyze the proportions of the face (smile line vs. pupil line, center of the face vs. center of the incisors, perpendicularity of the teeth to the horizon of the lips). Only then do we propose specific dimensions and color. It’s a process — not a quick decision made during the first visit.
estetyczna.dsdIntro: DSD is digital smile planning before we do anything irreversible. The patient sees the final effect on the screen + in the mouth (composite mock-up) before accepting the plan. This eliminates the element of surprise — you know how you will look.
estetyczna.dsdStep1Text: A series of photos taken with a DSLR camera using retractors and a Vita 3D-Master colorimeter. The photos capture color, proportions, and asymmetries — under controlled lighting conditions. Additionally: portrait photos with and without a smile. This is the database for planning.
estetyczna.dsdStep2Text: A 3D scanner builds a digital model of the teeth. We combine it with portrait photos in special software. We analyze: smile line, center of the face, proportions of the incisors to the face (golden ratio), color of adjacent teeth.
estetyczna.dsdStep3Title: Virtual Mockup + Physical Trial in the Mouth
estetyczna.dsdStep3Text: We design the target teeth digitally — showing the patient on the screen. Then from this design, we create a composite mock-up: temporary restorations placed on the teeth without grinding. The patient wears this for 1-2 days, evaluates it in the mirror, and shows it to family.
estetyczna.dsdStep4Text: If the mock-up is liked — we transfer it to the final material (composite, porcelain). If something needs to be changed — we modify and do a second trial. Only after full acceptance do we begin irreversible work. The patient knows the final cost before the first grind.
estetyczna.materialsIntro: Four main paths of aesthetics — from least to most invasive. Each has its indications, limitations, and durability period. The choice depends on the initial condition of the teeth and the patient's goals.
estetyczna.material1Text: Direct restoration with light-curing composite. The doctor layers the material, shaping it manually under a microscope. No laboratory, no temporary crown — the effect is immediate.
estetyczna.material1WhenToUse: Ideal for: diastema (gap between incisors), minor chipping, shape correction of incisors
estetyczna.material2Text: A larger scope than bonding — we cover the entire front surface of the tooth with composite. Color and shape control is better than in bonding, but durability is lower than in porcelain. Easy to correct without replacing the entire veneer.
estetyczna.material2WhenToUse: Good for: superficial discolorations, shape asymmetries, correction of 4-6 teeth at once
estetyczna.material3Text: Thin ceramic shells (0.3-0.7 mm) bonded to the prepared surface of the tooth. E.max (lithium disilicate, Empress) — a material with transparency similar to natural enamel. Made individually in a dental technician's laboratory.
estetyczna.material3WhenToUse: Best for: complete aesthetic reconstruction of 6-10 teeth, large irregularities, severe discolorations (e.g., from tetracyclines)
estetyczna.material4Text: Complete coverage of the tooth crown with ceramic (E.max press / zirconia). Used when the tooth is severely damaged, after root canal treatment, or as part of implant work. The most durable aesthetic solution, but requiring more grinding.
estetyczna.material4WhenToUse: Essential for: teeth after RCT with large defects, aesthetic bridges, crowns on implants in the aesthetic zone
estetyczna.decisionTitle: When to Choose Which Solution? — Decision Tree
estetyczna.decisionIntro: Patients often ask whether bonding is enough or if they need veneers. The answer depends on the specific clinical situation. Here are the key scenarios:
estetyczna.decisionCase1When: Diastema 1-3 mm, healthy teeth, good color
estetyczna.decisionCase1Then: Bonding — the cheapest, fastest, reversible. We perform it in 1-2 hours on 2-4 teeth.
estetyczna.decisionCase2When: Mild discolorations from coffee/wine, general lack of whiteness
estetyczna.decisionCase2Then: Whitening first, assess the effect after 2 weeks. If it's enough — end. If not — bonding/veneers on specific teeth.
estetyczna.decisionCase3When: Large shape irregularities, asymmetries, correction of 6+ teeth
estetyczna.decisionCase3Then: Veneers — composite (1 visit) or porcelain (2-3 visits). Porcelain is better aesthetically and more durable, but more expensive.
estetyczna.decisionCase4When: Tooth after root canal treatment, large defect, dark discoloration
estetyczna.decisionCase4Then: Full-ceramic E.max crown. A veneer alone is not enough — the tooth is weakened and requires full coverage.
estetyczna.decisionCase5When: Tetracyclines (severe discolorations from childhood), lack of whiteness after whitening
estetyczna.decisionCase5Then: Thick E.max porcelain veneers (0.7-1 mm) — bonding/composite will not hide such discolorations.
estetyczna.workflowIntro: Every aesthetic procedure has a standard protocol. Below are the steps for veneers/bonding under a microscope — the protocol complies with the standards of modern adhesive dentistry:
estetyczna.workflowStep1Text: Computer-controlled infiltration anesthesia — no stiff lip effect, no painful injection. Often, we perform bonding/composite veneers without anesthesia at all (minimal invasion in enamel).
estetyczna.workflowStep2Text: A rubber sheet isolates the teeth from the rest of the mouth. A dry working field = adhesion of the composite/cement works correctly. Without a rubber dam, durability decreases by 30-40% (scientific literature).
estetyczna.workflowStep3Text: Magnification of 4-25× allows for PRECISE grinding — we remove only as much enamel as really needed (typically 0.3-0.5 mm). Without a microscope, the doctor over-grinds to be sure — this is an irreversible loss of healthy enamel.
estetyczna.workflowStep4Text: Etching with 37% phosphoric acid, primer + bond, layered application of composite (each layer max 2 mm, cured with a polymerization lamp). Cervical, dentin, enamel layer — the effect of depth of color like in a natural tooth.
estetyczna.workflowStep5Text: Hand modeling of the surface under a microscope — we simulate the microanatomy of a natural tooth (grooves, micro-fissures, incisal edges). Polishing with diamond pastes to a shine comparable to enamel.
estetyczna.workflowStep6Text: After the procedure, a series of post-operative photographs — we document the effect + compare it with the initial state. A follow-up visit after 2 weeks — assessment of adaptation, possible minor corrections.
estetyczna.whiteningIntro: Whitening is often the first step before more invasive aesthetic procedures. The brighter the initial teeth, the less needs to be corrected later with bonding/veneers. Two methods — chosen individually:
estetyczna.whitening_inOffice_text: Professional whitening agents (hydrogen peroxide 35-40%) applied to the teeth under the doctor's supervision. Procedure time: 60-90 min, effect up to 6-8 shades lighter in one session. We use light activation.
estetyczna.whitening_inOffice_best: Best when: you want a quick effect, have a specific date (wedding, photo), moderate discolorations
estetyczna.whitening_home_text: Custom trays (silicone splints) with whitening gel (carbamide peroxide 10-22%). The patient wears them at night or for 2-4 hours a day for 2-4 weeks. The pace and intensity are controlled by the patient.
estetyczna.whitening_home_best: Best when: tooth sensitivity (gentler preparation), long-term maintenance of effect, pace control
estetyczna.whiteningSensitivity: Note on sensitivity: after whitening (any type), some patients experience temporary sensitivity to cold, lasting 1-3 days. We use fluoride + desensitizing pastes. Sensitivity resolves spontaneously. For patients with existing sensitivity, we recommend at-home tray whitening (gentler).
estetyczna.expertiseText1: M.Sc. in Lasers in Dentistry (RWTH Aachen, 2021 with distinction) directly translates to daily work with aesthetic restoration. The Er:YAG laser allows for clinical crown lengthening during the restoration procedure — without bleeding, without waiting for gum healing, without a second visit.
estetyczna.expertiseText2: In practice: A patient comes in with a broken incisor below the gum line. In the classic approach — gingivectomy with a scalpel, bleeding, impossible isolation, a second visit after 2 weeks. With us — the Er:YAG laser (MSP 1.5W, 15Hz) lengthens the crown and controls hemostasis in 30 seconds. Isolation is ready, restoration is performed in the same session.
estetyczna.expertiseText3: The second element is the Nd:YAG laser (1064nm) — photobiostimulation of root dentin. It eliminates the need for aggressive disinfection with hypochlorite during the restoration of teeth after root canal treatment. Plus LLLT therapy (Low Level Light Therapy) after the procedure — most of our patients do not need pain relief medications after aesthetic work.
estetyczna.expertiseText4: These are not gadgets. These are tools with specific protocols that I learned at RWTH Aachen and which we have been using in the office for years. I lectured about them at the LA&HA Symposium (Slovenia 2019, 2023; Poland 2022 — keynote). A full list of publications and accreditations is available on the team’s page.
estetyczna.durabilityTitle: Durability and Care — What to Do to Keep the Effect for Years
estetyczna.durabilityIntro: All aesthetic restorations have declared durability with proper hygiene. Bonding 5-7 years, composite veneers 7-10, porcelain 15-20, E.max crowns 15-25. The most common mistakes that shorten this time:
estetyczna.durabilityTip1: Home hygiene: brushing with a sonic toothbrush 2× a day + dental floss + irrigator. Without this, bacterial plaque undermines the edges of the restoration and fillings lose their tightness.
estetyczna.durabilityTip2: Hygienization in the office: every 6 months — removal of tartar + polishing. Without this, veneers lose their shine, composites discolor from coffee/wine.
estetyczna.durabilityTip3: Bruxism (teeth grinding at night): if you have it — use a relaxation splint. Bonding/composite veneers crack from bruxist biting forces in 2-3 years instead of 7-10.
estetyczna.durabilityTip4: Diet choices: coffee, wine, tea, cigarettes stain EVERYTHING (teeth, composites, veneers). E.max porcelain is the most resistant. After coffee — rinse with water.
estetyczna.durabilityTip5: Contact sports: get a custom dental guard (mouthguard). A broken incisor with a porcelain veneer = replacement of the entire veneer (~2500 PLN). Composite can be refilled, porcelain cannot be glued.
estetyczna.crossLinkTitle: Bigger Plan? Aesthetics is Often Part of a Broader Program
estetyczna.crossLinkText: If a tooth requires prior root canal treatment (bad old filling, crack reaching the pulp) — we first perform RCT under a microscope, then aesthetic restoration. If there are missing teeth in the aesthetic zone — implantology + full-ceramic crown. The full plan starts with a consultation with photography + CBCT.
estetyczna.crossLinkProtetyka: See prosthetics — crowns, bridges, full rehabilitation
estetyczna.crossLinkEndo: See root canal treatment — when a tooth requires RCT before restoration
estetyczna.faqA5: No — provided they are done correctly. The grind for a porcelain veneer is 0.3-0.7 mm only from the front surface (80%+ healthy enamel remains). Bonding and composite veneers can be done without any grinding at all. The myth about damaging teeth with veneers comes from the 90s when 1-1.5 mm was ground and healthy incisors were turned into stumps for crowns.
estetyczna.faqQ6: Can I have veneers if I have gum disease?
estetyczna.faqA6: No — we first treat the gums. Aesthetics on an inflammatory basis will last 1-2 years instead of 15. After treatment (hygienization + possibly subgingival scaling + home hygiene instruction), we return to the aesthetic plan. From my experience: a 6-8 week break between hygienization and aesthetics is sufficient in 90% of cases.
estetyczna.faqA7: No — hydrogen peroxide at office concentrations (35-40%) does not affect the structure of enamel. Long-term studies (10+ years) do not confirm this. Sensitivity after the procedure is temporary (1-3 days) and does not indicate damage. What CAN harm: at-home whitening without consultation (e.g., baking soda, charcoal paste mechanically abrades enamel).
estetyczna.faqQ8: Can I drink coffee after whitening?
estetyczna.faqA8: For the first 48 hours no — the pores of the enamel are open, risking strong discolorations. After 48 hours normal drinking is fine, but limiting chromogenic liquids (coffee, wine, tea, carrot juice) extends the effect. A straw for coffee helps (bypassing the front row of teeth).
estetyczna.faqQ9: Is bonding enough instead of a veneer?
estetyczna.faqA9: Often yes — especially for diastemas up to 3 mm, minor chipping, shape correction of the incisor. Bonding is cheaper (~500 PLN/tooth), faster (1 visit), reversible. Disadvantages: durability 5-7 years (vs. 15-20 for porcelain), sensitivity to dyes, more prone to chipping. The decision will be made during the consultation after photographic analysis.

ortodoncja.title: Orthodontics Opole
ortodoncja.subtitle: Clear Correct transparent aligners
ortodoncja.intro1: Do you dream of straight teeth but don't want metal braces? At {brandName}, we offer orthodontic treatment with Clear Correct transparent aligners — virtually invisible on your teeth.
ortodoncja.intro2: During your first visit, we will perform a 3D scan and show you a simulation of the final result. You will know exactly how your teeth will look after treatment and how long it will take.
ortodoncja.step1Text: Digital scan of your teeth and simulation of the final result. You will know exactly what to expect.
ortodoncja.step2Text: A set of transparent aligners designed specifically for you, changed every 1-2 weeks.
ortodoncja.step3Text: You wear them for at least 22 hours a day, removing them only for eating and brushing. They are virtually invisible.
ortodoncja.step4Text: After treatment, we place a retainer (a thin wire) that keeps the result forever.
ortodoncja.benefit1: Virtually invisible — no one will notice you are straightening your teeth.
ortodoncja.benefit2: Removable — you can eat, drink, and brush your teeth normally.
ortodoncja.benefit3: Comfortable — no wires or brackets that irritate your cheeks.
ortodoncja.benefit4: Shorter treatment time — on average 6-18 months.
ortodoncja.benefit5: 3D simulation — see the final result before you start.
ortodoncja.faqQ1: How do Clear Correct aligners work?
ortodoncja.faqA1: A set of transparent trays changed every 1-2 weeks. Each one gently shifts your teeth into the correct position.
ortodoncja.faqQ2: Does straightening teeth with aligners hurt?
ortodoncja.faqA2: The pain is minimal — a feeling of pressure for 1-2 days after a new aligner. Incomparably less than with metal braces.
ortodoncja.faqQ3: How long does the treatment take?
ortodoncja.faqA3: On average 6-18 months, depending on the issue. We will provide the exact time during your first visit.
ortodoncja.faqQ4: Will my teeth go back to their old position?
ortodoncja.faqA4: No — we use retention (a wire on the inside or a night aligner) that guarantees the durability of the result.
ortodoncja.intro3: Most Patients who come to us requesting orthodontics are aged 25-45 — they work in professions where visibility is part of the job (sales, presentations, client contact). For them, traditional metal braces with brackets and wires are unacceptable. Clear Correct solves this problem — the aligners are virtually invisible and removable during meals or important meetings.
ortodoncja.philosophyText1: Orthodontics is not just about aesthetics. Crooked teeth often lead to functional problems: an asymmetric bite overloads one temporomandibular joint (TMJ), making hygiene more difficult → faster cavities, some contact points undergo excessive wear (attrition). Correcting the bite at ages 30-40 is an investment in 30 more years of oral health.
ortodoncja.philosophyText2: At the same time, not everyone needs orthodontics. Slight aesthetic irregularities can often be resolved with bonding or veneers in one session (instead of 12 months of aligners). The decision: orthodontics vs conservative aesthetics vs a combination of both — requires consultation and analysis. This is a topic for an honest conversation, not an automatic referral.
ortodoncja.philosophyText3: Important: orthodontics can be a stage before aesthetics. First, we correct the alignment of the teeth (orthodontics), then we refine the color and shape (bonding or veneers). Such sequencing gives the most durable effect — because veneers on crooked teeth will eventually crack due to uneven bite forces.
ortodoncja.howIntro: Standard Clear Correct protocol in our office. Each of the 4 stages is documented — the Patient knows exactly what is happening and when.
ortodoncja.comparisonTitle: Clear Correct vs Invisalign vs Fixed Braces
ortodoncja.comparisonIntro: Three main paths of orthodontics for adults. The choice depends on the complexity of the defect, budget, and aesthetic preferences.
ortodoncja.method1Text: The most popular solution for adults. Removable aligners = normal hygiene and eating. Discipline required: minimum 22 hours a day of wear. Price is intermediate between fixed braces and Invisalign.
ortodoncja.method1When: Best for: defects in up to 80% of cases in adults, complete invisibility
ortodoncja.method2Text: Pioneer of the category — serving a larger range of patients (extreme defects). Higher price than Clear Correct. Similar discipline requirements. Often chosen for more complicated cases.
ortodoncja.method2When: Best for: very large bite defects where Clear Correct is insufficient
ortodoncja.method3Text: Traditional, best orthodontic control — full control of tooth movement. But: visibility, more difficult hygiene, risk of enamel changes. Rarely chosen by adults — mainly in adolescents or very difficult cases.
ortodoncja.method3When: Best for: complicated bone defects requiring precise control
ortodoncja.timelineIntro: Treatment time mainly depends on the size of the initial defect. Below are typical scenarios we observe in the office:
ortodoncja.timelineCase1When: Slight irregularity of the lower incisors
ortodoncja.timelineCase1Then: Clear Correct 4-6 months, 10-15 aligners. A common case among young adults.
ortodoncja.timelineCase2When: Moderate crowding (both arches, lack of space for incisors)
ortodoncja.timelineCase2Then: Clear Correct 8-12 months, 20-30 aligners. May require IPR (interproximal reduction — minimal grinding of 0.1-0.2 mm between teeth).
ortodoncja.timelineCase3When: Large bite defect, Class II/III, open/reverse
ortodoncja.timelineCase3Then: Clear Correct 18-24 months, 40-50 aligners + frequent attachments. In extreme cases: referral for Invisalign or fixed braces.
ortodoncja.timelineCase4When: Only retention after previous treatment (teeth have shifted)
ortodoncja.timelineCase4Then: Re-Align — 3-6 months, 5-10 aligners. Often sufficient to restore the effect from years ago.
ortodoncja.retentionTitle: Retention After Treatment — The Most Important Stage
ortodoncja.retentionIntro: The most common mistake Patients after orthodontics make: neglecting retention because the teeth look good already. Result: after 2-3 years, the teeth return to their original position. Retention is forever. Here’s how we do it:
ortodoncja.retentionPoint1: Retention wire glued on the inside (lingually) of the lower and upper incisors — invisible, worn constantly. Replacement every 5-10 years (when it detaches or rusts).
ortodoncja.retentionPoint2: Retention splint worn at night — for the first 6 months daily, then 2-3 times a week. Signals the teeth to stay in place.
ortodoncja.retentionPoint3: Check-ups every 12 months — assessing whether retention is working, whether the wire is holding, and whether the splint needs replacement.
ortodoncja.kidsText1: In children, early intervention can often save full orthodontics at ages 16-18. Example: a palatal expander at ages 7-9 widens the upper arch and makes room for incisors — without the need for premolar extractions later.
ortodoncja.kidsText2: Other early interventions: functional appliances (Class II/III), mini-screws for jaw growth adjustment, reflex blockers (finger in mouth, mouth breathing). Orthodontics in children does not always mean full braces — often a small correction at the right stage of development is sufficient.
ortodoncja.kidsText3: The first orthodontic visit — recommended at age 7 (Polish Orthodontic Society). Early assessment allows planning any potential intervention at the appropriate stage of development.
ortodoncja.benefit6: 3D simulation capability — see the final effect before making a decision.
ortodoncja.benefit7: Minimal conflict with fixed braces — you can work in client-facing jobs without embarrassment.
ortodoncja.marcinText1: Openness: Marcin is not an orthodontist. In our office, we conduct Clear Correct orthodontic treatment (simple and moderate defects), and for complicated cases, we refer to a dedicated orthodontist we have collaborated with for years. It’s a matter of honesty — it’s not worth treating outside of one’s specialty.
ortodoncja.marcinText2: What Marcin does before/after orthodontics: comprehensive aesthetic analysis (combined with DSD), bonding or composite veneers after orthodontic treatment (aesthetic refinement), and microscopic inspection of the enamel surface after removing Clear Correct attachments (the enamel surface after adhesives often requires polishing).
ortodoncja.marcinText3: From the perspective of M.Sc. RWTH Aachen — Er:YAG laser allows for precise removal of adhesive residue after removing fixed braces/Clear Correct attachments without the risk of damaging the enamel. This is the finishing touch that most offices overlook — and it makes a difference for the long-term appearance of the teeth.
ortodoncja.crossLinkTitle: Orthodontics is Often a Stage of a Broader Plan
ortodoncja.crossLinkText: After completing orthodontic treatment, it is worth refining aesthetics (color, minor shape corrections with bonding). Sometimes orthodontics requires prior extraction of premolars (we refer to surgery).
ortodoncja.crossLinkEstetyczna: See aesthetic dentistry — bonding/veneers after orthodontics
ortodoncja.crossLinkChirurgia: See surgery — when extraction is needed before orthodontics
ortodoncja.faqQ5: Can I drink coffee with Clear Correct aligners?
ortodoncja.faqA5: No — coffee, wine, tea stain the aligners in 2-3 days. Remove the aligners before every meal and drink (except water). This is an additional restriction — but at the same time a limit for unhealthy snacks between meals.
ortodoncja.faqQ6: Can I play sports with Clear Correct?
ortodoncja.faqA6: Yes — the aligners do not limit sports. For contact sports (boxing, MMA, rugby), we recommend a protective mouthguard during the fight/match. Swimming, running, gym — without restrictions.
ortodoncja.faqA7: The first 1-3 days after a new aligner — feeling of pressure, slight sensitivity. It passes on its own. Incomparably less than fixed braces (where after tightening it hurts for 3-5 days). Most Patients do not take painkillers.
ortodoncja.faqA8: You can — but the effect will be partial. After stopping, the teeth remain in their current position (they do not return to the original position — provided immediate retention is applied). Practical advice: do not start treatment if you are not 100% ready for 6-18 months of discipline.
ortodoncja.faqQ9: Does Clear Correct solve all defects?
ortodoncja.faqA9: No — in 15-20% of cases, the defect is too complicated and Clear Correct is insufficient. Typical examples: very large Class II/III, requiring significant extraction, skeletal disorders requiring orthognathic surgery. In such cases, we honestly refer to an orthodontist with fixed braces or to Invisalign Lite.

chirurgia.title: Dental Surgery Opole
chirurgia.subtitle: Tooth Extraction — Wisdom Teeth — PRF
chirurgia.intro1: Surgery at {brandName} is not just about tooth extraction. It involves precise procedures using the latest technologies — PRF (Platelet-Rich Fibrin), laser, ozone therapy — which accelerate healing and minimize discomfort.
chirurgia.intro2: Each surgical procedure is preceded by tomographic diagnostics (CBCT), allowing us to know exactly what to expect even before the procedure begins.
chirurgia.service1Text: Painless removal of wisdom teeth, including those completely impacted in the bone. CBCT diagnostics + PRF = quick healing.
chirurgia.service2Text: Atraumatic removal of teeth with maximum preservation of bone and gum for future implantation.
chirurgia.service3Text: Bone reconstruction with bone substitute material prior to implantation. Sinus Lift.
chirurgia.service4Text: Gum plastic surgery, recessions, vestibule deepening. Aesthetics and function of soft tissues.
chirurgia.prfText: PRF (Platelet-Rich Fibrin) is a platelet-rich fibrin obtained from your blood. It acts like a natural super-bandage — accelerating healing by several times, reducing swelling and pain after the procedure. We use it routinely with every extraction.
chirurgia.faqQ1: When should wisdom teeth be removed?
chirurgia.faqA1: When there is not enough space for them, they cause inflammation, cavities in neighboring teeth, or cysts. We assess this through a panoramic X-ray.
chirurgia.faqA2: Platelet-rich fibrin from your blood. It acts like a natural bandage, accelerating wound healing by several times.
chirurgia.faqQ3: What are the recommendations after tooth extraction?
chirurgia.faqA3: 2 hours without eating. Avoid hot meals and strenuous activity. Do not rinse your mouth vigorously. Use cold compresses.
chirurgia.faqQ4: Is dry socket a common complication?
chirurgia.faqA4: It occurs rarely (about 2-5%). By using PRF and ozone therapy, we drastically reduce the risk of complications.
chirurgia.intro3: Most Patients fear surgery because they associate it with painful childhood visits: bleeding, swelling, two weeks of suffering. Modern surgery is a different world — computer anesthesia The Wand, PRF (fibrin from your own blood) for the wound, laser biostimulation LLLT after the procedure. Most Patients do not need pain medications stronger than ibuprofen.
chirurgia.scopeTitle: What we do and what we don't
chirurgia.scopeText1: In our office, we perform: extractions (simple and atypical, including impacted wisdom teeth), apicoectomy (microsurgery endodontics), single and bridge implants, bone augmentations (sinus lift, GBR), procedures with PRF and ozone therapy.
chirurgia.scopeText2: What we do not do: major orthognathic surgery (jaw-jaw class), oncological, traumatic. This requires hospital conditions and specialists with dedicated residency. We refer to partner hospital clinics in Opole/Wrocław.
chirurgia.scopeText3: Our philosophy: if I can perform the procedure safely and predictably — we do it in the office. If it requires hospital conditions — we refer honestly. It is not an art to try to do everything ourselves — the art is knowing when it is not our case.
chirurgia.servicesIntro: The range of surgical procedures performed in the office. Each preceded by CBCT diagnostics (tomography) — we know exactly what to expect before the incision.
chirurgia.service5Text: Resection of the root apex + retrograde fill. A rescue for teeth with recurrent infection after RCT. Performed with Er:YAG laser — minimal invasion, quick healing.
chirurgia.service6Text: Correction of a too short frenulum of the lip or tongue. In children: speech problems, sucking. In adults: gum recession. Er:YAG laser = no bleeding, no stitches.
chirurgia.wisdomIntro: Wisdom teeth (third molars) are the most common surgical procedure in the office. The procedure is predictable and safe provided that accurate diagnostics are performed.
chirurgia.wisdomStep1Text: Before each extraction of an impacted wisdom tooth, we perform CBCT. Assessment: root position vs. inferior alveolar nerve, angle of impaction, condition of the crown, risk of paresthesia (numbness of the lip). Without CBCT, it is working in the dark — we do not undertake it.
chirurgia.wisdomStep2Title: The Wand anesthesia + sedation if needed
chirurgia.wisdomStep2Text: Computerized infiltration anesthesia + conduction — without the effect of a stiff half of the face. For Patients with increased anxiety, we offer inhalation sedation with nitrous oxide (laughing gas, conscious but relaxed).
chirurgia.wisdomStep3Text: Extraction with minimal invasion (sometimes root sectioning for impacted wisdom teeth). PRF (fibrin from the Patient's blood) introduced into the socket — accelerates healing 2-3×. Resorbable stitches (disappear spontaneously after 10-14 days, no visit for removal).
chirurgia.wisdomStep4Text: Immediately after the procedure — Nd:YAG laser in LLLT (Low Level Light Therapy) mode on the surgical area. Reduces postoperative pain + accelerates healing. The Patient receives written recommendations + our number 24/7 in case of questions.
chirurgia.prfText1: PRF (Platelet Rich Fibrin) is a membrane produced from the Patient's blood just before the procedure. We take 10-20 ml of blood, spin it in a special centrifuge — in 12 minutes we obtain a yellowish gelatinous membrane rich in platelets and growth factors.
chirurgia.prfText2: The membrane is introduced directly into the wound after extraction or during bone augmentation. Platelets release growth factors (PDGF, VEGF, TGF-beta) that actively stimulate tissue regeneration. Without PRF, the wound heals in 2-3 weeks — with PRF usually 7-10 days.
chirurgia.prfText3: 100% natural — no chemical additives, no risk of allergic reactions. The material comes from your own blood. The procedure is approved by most European surgical societies. In our office, we routinely offer PRF during wisdom tooth extractions + any implant placement.
chirurgia.prfUse1: After extraction of impacted wisdom teeth (especially lower ones) — reduction of swelling and postoperative pain
chirurgia.prfUse2: Any implant placement — faster integration of the implant with the bone, lower risk of complications
chirurgia.prfUse3: Bone augmentation / sinus lift — PRF as a component of biomaterial for GBR (Guided Bone Regeneration)
chirurgia.prfUse4: Patients with diabetes, smokers, steroid medications — any situations where healing is worse
chirurgia.apicoText1: Apicoectomy is a surgical rescue of a tooth after unsuccessful root canal treatment. Standard sequence: when conventional Re-Endo (retreatment under a microscope) is not enough — we perform surgical removal of the root apex + retrograde fill (closure of the root from the surgical side).
chirurgia.apicoText2: We perform it with an Er:YAG laser (with procedural documentation from my master's thesis at RWTH Aachen). The laser allows for precise incision of the bone + minimal invasion of the periapical tissue. Minimal bleeding. Resorbable stitches. We observe healing radiologically for 6-12 months.
chirurgia.apicoText3: Effectiveness: 70-85% with appropriate qualification (quote from literature, consistent with our results). This is a discipline that Marcin has been working in for years — we combine it with our core microscopic profile (crown imaging, searching for missed canals, laser disinfection).
chirurgia.adjunctIntro: In addition to traditional surgery, we use 3 supporting technologies that accelerate healing and reduce discomfort:
chirurgia.adjunct_ozone_text: Ozone (O3) has strong disinfecting properties — eliminates bacteria, viruses, and fungi without using antibiotics. We apply it to the socket after extraction and around the surgical site. It reduces the risk of infection and dry socket.
chirurgia.adjunct_laser_text: Er:YAG laser (2940 nm) replaces the scalpel for gingival incisions. Advantages: no bleeding (the laser cuts and coagulates simultaneously), no stitches in many cases (the wound closes spontaneously), 2× faster healing of soft tissues.
chirurgia.adjunct_lllt_text: Low Level Light Therapy — low power Nd:YAG laser (0.5W) applied immediately after the procedure. Induces regenerative processes at the cellular level. Patients report significantly less postoperative pain and lower medication consumption.
chirurgia.postopIntro: Standard recommendations after a surgical procedure. Each Patient receives a written copy + our phone number 24/7 in case of questions. Most recoveries are trouble-free.
chirurgia.postop1: 2 hours without eating/drinking — allow the clot to stabilize. No hot drinks for 24 hours.
chirurgia.postop2: Cold compresses for 15 minutes every 1-2 hours for the first 24 hours — drastically reduce swelling. After 24 hours, switch to warm (accelerate circulation).
chirurgia.postop3: No vigorous rinsing for 48 hours — the clot may detach → risk of dry socket. After 24 hours, gently rinse with saline.
chirurgia.postop4: Medications: ibuprofen 400 mg every 6 hours for the first 48 hours prophylactically. Most Patients after PRF + LLLT do not need stronger medications.
chirurgia.postop5: No smoking for 72 hours — nicotine increases the risk of complications by 3-4×. No alcohol for 48 hours (interaction with medications).
chirurgia.expertiseText1: Title: Oral Surgery Academy Graduate (specialized dental surgery program). Plus M.Sc. RWTH Aachen in laser dentistry. These two elements combine into protocols that we use: Er:YAG laser for incisions (minimal invasion), Nd:YAG for hemostasis and LLLT, PRF for every major extraction.
chirurgia.expertiseText2: In practice: extraction of a wisdom tooth typically requires a scalpel, forceps, chisel, stitches, 2 weeks of healing with pain. With us: Er:YAG laser instead of scalpel, PRF instead of an empty socket, LLLT after the procedure. The Patient returns to work the next day without pain medications. This is not advertising — it is the standard of our office.
chirurgia.expertiseText3: Lectures at the LA&HA Symposium (Slovenia 2019, 2023 + Poland 2022 keynote 'From Root to Crown — Comprehensive Laser Treatment of Teeth'). I teach this protocol to other dentists at international conferences. Every Patient benefits from this knowledge.
chirurgia.crossLinkTitle: Surgery often leads to the next stage
chirurgia.crossLinkText: After extraction, it is worth considering an implant instead of waiting for the bone to resorb (2-3 mm per year). Apicoectomy is a surgical rescue after unsuccessful root canal treatment — first check if Re-Endo under a microscope is sufficient.
chirurgia.crossLinkImplant: See implantology — implant instead of leaving a gap
chirurgia.crossLinkEndo: See root canal treatment — Re-Endo before apicoectomy
chirurgia.faqQ5: Is wisdom tooth extraction a complicated procedure?
chirurgia.faqA5: It depends on the position of the wisdom tooth. If erupted — it is a simple extraction, 20-30 minutes. If impacted (horizontal, mesial, deeply embedded in the bone) — 45-90 minutes with root sectioning. Decision after CBCT. Safety is always the most important: when the risk of paresthesia is high, we refer to maxillofacial surgery.
chirurgia.faqQ6: How much pain is there after the procedure?
chirurgia.faqA6: Most Patients report moderate discomfort for the first 24-48 hours, controlled with ibuprofen 400 mg. Thanks to PRF and LLLT, postoperative pain is significantly reduced compared to traditional surgery. After 3 days, discomfort is typically minimal.
chirurgia.faqQ7: Can I drive after the procedure?
chirurgia.faqA7: After a standard extraction with local anesthesia — yes, right after the procedure. After inhalation sedation (nitrous oxide) — no, please come with an accompanying person. After more extensive procedures (apicoectomy, implant + augmentation) — we recommend resting that day.
chirurgia.faqQ8: How long does complete healing take?
chirurgia.faqA8: After a simple extraction: 7-10 days for the gum to close, 6-8 weeks for full bone regeneration. After PRF, soft tissue healing is reduced to 5-7 days. After implantation: 3-4 months of osseointegration before loading with a crown.
chirurgia.faqQ9: Can I avoid wisdom tooth extraction?
chirurgia.faqA9: Yes, if the wisdom tooth is properly erupted, has contact with the opposing tooth (functions in occlusion), does not cause crowding, and is maintained hygienically. Prophylactic extraction 'because everyone removes them' is an outdated philosophy. Decision after individual assessment + CBCT.

protetyka.title: Prosthetics Opole
protetyka.subtitle: Crowns — Bridges — Dentures
protetyka.intro1: Prosthetics at {brandName} combines the highest aesthetics with durability. We use only the highest quality materials — E.max porcelain, zirconia, hybrid composites — to ensure your restoration is indistinguishable from natural teeth.
protetyka.intro2: With the 3D digital scanner, we say goodbye to unpleasant alginate impressions. Scanning is quick, convenient, and provides a more accurate result.
protetyka.type1Text: E.max and zirconia crowns — the highest aesthetics, no gum discoloration. Indistinguishable from a natural tooth.
protetyka.type2Text: Restoration of missing teeth. Traditional and adhesive bridges (on fiberglass, without grinding neighboring teeth).
protetyka.type3Text: Porcelain inlays — an alternative to large fillings. More durable and aesthetic than composite fillings.
protetyka.type4Text: Framework and acrylic dentures. A solution for extensive tooth loss when implants are not possible.
protetyka.digitalText: Instead of unpleasant impression material, we use a 3D scanner. The camera scans the teeth in a few minutes, and the precise digital model is sent to the laboratory. The finished prosthetic work is usually cemented after 5-7 working days.
protetyka.faqQ1: Crown or veneer — what to choose?
protetyka.faqA1: A veneer covers only the front part of the tooth (aesthetics). A crown encompasses the entire tooth (reinforcement). The decision depends on the degree of damage.
protetyka.faqQ2: What materials do you use for crowns?
protetyka.faqA2: Mainly full ceramic E.max and zirconia crowns. They do not cause gum discoloration and are indistinguishable from a natural tooth.
protetyka.faqQ3: What does the crown-making process look like?
protetyka.faqA3: We scan the teeth with a 3D camera (no impressions!), send the design to the laboratory. The finished crown is cemented after 5-7 days.
protetyka.faqA4: Adhesive bridges on fiberglass. They allow for the restoration of a missing tooth without heavily grinding neighboring teeth.
protetyka.crossLinkTitle: Missing a tooth? An implant is often the best choice
protetyka.crossLinkDesc: A crown mounted on an implant replaces the missing tooth without grinding down the neighbours. A durable 1:1 solution instead of a bridge resting on adjacent teeth.
protetyka.intro3: Modern prosthetics is digital — we use the 3Shape Trios intraoral scanner instead of traditional impression materials. Less discomfort (no biting into silicone for 5 minutes), more precise reproduction (the scanner measures in microns), faster workflow (the scan is sent digitally to the lab in seconds, no waiting for plaster transport).
protetyka.philosophyTitle: The philosophy of prosthetics in our clinic
protetyka.philosophyText1: Prosthetics is the last element in the hierarchy of treatment — we perform it when conservative and endodontic options have been exhausted or when aesthetics require exceeding what bonding/veneers can provide. Philosophy: preserve as many of your own teeth as possible, and where it is impossible — restore in a way that looks and functions like a natural tooth.
protetyka.philosophyText2: Every plan begins with a comprehensive analysis: the condition of remaining teeth, occlusion, aesthetics, gum health, and caries risk profile. Sometimes the best prosthetic decision is to DELAY prosthetics for 6 months — to first treat periodontitis, orthodontics, or RCT of problematic teeth. In the meantime, we use temporary restorations.
protetyka.philosophyText3: What good prosthetics mean in 2026: digital workflow from scan to CAM milling, next-generation materials (E.max press, translucent zirconia), precise fitting under a ZEISS microscope, photographic color documentation. Work equal to dental technician + dentist — without compromises on either side.
protetyka.typesIntro: Six main types of prosthetic restorations — from least to most complex. Each has its clinical and aesthetic indications.
protetyka.type5Text: Partial tooth restoration — when the cavity is too large for a filling but too small for a full crown. Material: E.max press or composite. Preserves more healthy enamel than a classic crown.
protetyka.type6Text: 3D printed temporary crowns during the treatment process. The patient does NOT walk around with an exposed stump between visits — they have aesthetically pleasing teeth throughout the treatment period. Made from scanner files during the same session.
protetyka.materialsIntro: Four main materials from which we make crowns and bridges. The choice depends on the position in the arch (incisor vs molar), the condition of remaining teeth, occlusal forces, and budget.
protetyka.material1Text: Translucent ceramic with optical properties similar to natural enamel. Best for anterior teeth (incisors, canines). Strong but not super-strong — in molars, there is slightly more risk of fracture than with zirconia.
protetyka.material2Text: Strength 3-4× higher than E.max. Best for lateral teeth (molars, premolars) and multi-unit bridges. Aesthetics slightly inferior to E.max — but by 2025, translucent zirconia is already very close to E.max visually.
protetyka.material3Text: Classic porcelain with the highest translucency — best for thin veneers 0.3-0.5 mm. Strength lower than E.max — mainly used in the aesthetic zone without lateral forces.
protetyka.material4Text: Traditional material — metal framework + ceramic layer. The most durable option for bridges with 3+ points. Drawbacks: black line at the gums after years (if the gum recedes), inability to make without a dark margin around the neck. By 2025, it is being replaced by translucent zirconia in most cases.
protetyka.digitalIntro: Complete prosthetic workflow in our clinic. Everything digital from the first visit.
protetyka.digitalStep1Text: The scanner builds a digital model in real-time — the patient sees their teeth on a 3D screen. No impression material, no discomfort, no gag reflex. Precision in microns. Time: 5-7 minutes for a full arch + mandible.
protetyka.digitalStep2Text: Files sent to a partner digital lab. The dental technician designs the crown/bridge in software (3Shape, exocad). We can view the design remotely and approve it before production. Iterations in case of corrections.
protetyka.digitalStep3Text: From a block of material (E.max, zirconia), the 5-axis mill cuts the crown with an accuracy of up to 5 microns. Then sintering at temperatures of 850-1500°C (depending on the material). Production time: 24-48 hours.
protetyka.digitalStep4Text: The technician performs manual characterization of color (shading, transparency, micro-etching of enamel) + glaze layer (shine). Each crown is individual — matching the neighboring teeth of the patient. This is where craftsmanship comes in.
protetyka.digitalStep5Text: Aesthetic try-in in the oral cavity — the patient assesses color, shape, occlusal contact. After approval — cementation with composite adhesive cement under a rubber dam (dry field = durability 2× higher than without isolation). Polishing of edges.
protetyka.decisionTitle: Decision tree — when to choose which solution
protetyka.decisionIntro: Patients often ask whether to get a bridge or an implant, or whether to choose an onlay or a crown. The answer depends on the clinical context. Here are key scenarios:
protetyka.decisionCase1When: Single missing tooth, adjacent teeth healthy
protetyka.decisionCase1Then: Implant + single crown. We do not grind healthy neighbors for a bridge — this is an outdated philosophy.
protetyka.decisionCase2When: Missing 2-3 adjacent teeth, adjacent teeth with large fillings
protetyka.decisionCase2Then: Bridge on 4-5 points (if adjacent teeth require crowns anyway) or 2-3 implants + bridge.
protetyka.decisionCase3When: Large cavity after RCT, tooth weakened, but walls preserved
protetyka.decisionCase3Then: Onlay/overlay E.max — preserves more tooth than a full crown. 2 visits.
protetyka.decisionCase4When: Missing many teeth + bone loss, no budget for implants
protetyka.decisionCase4Then: Skeletal denture with metal clasps (clamps) or telescopic (aesthetic).
protetyka.decisionCase5Then: All-on-4/6 (bridge on 4-6 implants with immediate loading). Life-changing solution — see implantology.
protetyka.durabilityIntro: All declared durability periods assume proper home hygiene + office check-ups every 6-12 months. The most common mistakes that shorten durability:
protetyka.durability1: Lack of dental floss / irrigator around crowns — bacterial plaque undermines the edges → secondary caries + leakage.
protetyka.durability2: Hygiene in the office every 6 months — removal of tartar + polishing of crown surfaces (porcelain loses shine over time).
protetyka.durability3: Bruxism (grinding teeth at night): absolutely a relaxation splint. Without this, crowns break in 2-3 years instead of 15-20.
protetyka.durability4: Hard foods: nuts in shells, ice, opening bottles with teeth. Each of these habits can shorten the durability of the crown by half.
protetyka.durability5: Contact sports: individual tooth protection (mouthguard). Mechanical trauma often breaks porcelain crowns beyond repair.
protetyka.expertiseText1: Work under the ZEISS Extaro microscope: every preparation for a crown, every edge of the preparation, every cementation — performed at 4-25× magnification. The edge of the preparation must be perfectly smooth and seamless — without this, the adhesive cement does not hold, the crown leaks, and caries return in 2-3 years.
protetyka.expertiseText2: Er:YAG laser for gum retraction before the impression: instead of traditional retraction cord (1-2 mm indentation in the gum + bleeding + long wait for hemostasis) — the laser removes a thin layer of gum opening the treatment field WITHOUT BLEEDING. Perfectly dry field = perfect impression. Technique from my master's thesis at RWTH Aachen.
protetyka.expertiseText3: Seamless preparation + 3D-print technique for temporary crown (from my article in the Dental Magazine 2020) — in one session we perform the preparation of the pillar + scan + design + print of the temporary crown. The patient leaves the office with an aesthetically pleasing temporary tooth, waiting 2-3 weeks for the final crown. Without an exposed stump between visits.
protetyka.crossLinkEndo: See root canal treatment — when a tooth requires RCT before a crown
protetyka.crossLinkEstetyczna: See aesthetic dentistry — DSD before full reconstruction
protetyka.faqQ5: Do you have to grind a healthy adjacent tooth to make a bridge?
protetyka.faqA5: Yes — with a classic bridge, adjacent teeth are pillars and require grinding for crowns. This is why an implant is a better choice for a single missing tooth — we do not touch healthy neighbors. A bridge makes sense mainly when adjacent teeth require crowns for other reasons (large fillings, RCT).
protetyka.faqQ6: How much does a full ceramic crown cost?
protetyka.faqA6: The price depends on the material, the technical lab, and the complexity of the case. Approximate range: E.max 1800-2500 PLN, zirconia 2000-2800 PLN, full porcelain Empress 2000-2500 PLN, metal-ceramic 1200-1600 PLN. Exact cost estimate after consultation + scan.
protetyka.faqQ7: Does the crown irritate the gum?
protetyka.faqA7: No — when properly made. The crown margin should be at or slightly below the gum level (subgingivally). Sharp edges or excess cement can irritate the gum — but that is a fault of the execution, not the crown itself. That’s why we work under a microscope and use rubber dam isolation during cementation.
protetyka.faqQ8: Can teeth under the crown still decay?
protetyka.faqA8: They can — this is called secondary caries and occurs when the crown edge becomes leaky (after years, with poor hygiene). Prevention: thorough dental flossing around crowns, hygiene every 6 months, X-ray check every 2 years. With our standard (microscope + rubber dam + appropriate cement) the risk of secondary caries < 5% in 10 years.
protetyka.faqQ9: Can an old crown be replaced?
protetyka.faqA9: Yes — an old crown is not a death sentence. If the edges are leaky, the color no longer matches, or the tooth underneath has caries — it can be replaced. Workflow: removal of the old crown (sometimes it needs to be cut), assessment of the tooth base, cleaning of caries if present, possibly RCT if deep, new crown from modern materials. Typically 2-3 visits.

allon4Opole.faqQ1: How much does All-on-4 cost in Opole?
allon4Opole.faqA1: All-on-4 starts at around 30,000-40,000 PLN per arch, All-on-6 is around 45,000-55,000 PLN. The price depends on the number of implants, the bridge material and any additional procedures. We offer instalments/financing, and give an exact quote after a consultation and CBCT scan.
allon4Opole.faqQ2: Will I get teeth in a single day?
allon4Opole.faqA2: In most cases, yes — with adequate implant stability we fit a temporary bridge within 24-48 h of surgery (immediate loading). The final zirconia bridge is made after healing, usually 3-6 months later.
allon4Opole.faqQ3: All-on-4 or All-on-6 — which is better?
allon4Opole.faqA3: We decide after a CBCT scan and bone assessment. All-on-6 gives better force distribution and more reserve (recommended with sufficient bone and bruxism), while All-on-4 suits cases with less bone. In extreme jaw atrophy we use zygomatic implants.
allon4Opole.faqA4: The procedure is performed under anesthesia, ensuring comfort. Temporary swelling and discomfort are possible afterwards, which we manage with medication; PRF supports faster healing. Most patients return to daily activities within a few days.
allon4Opole.faqQ5: Is there a warranty on All-on-4?
allon4Opole.faqA5: Yes — we provide a warranty on implant treatment. The screw-retained zirconia bridge is durable and wear-resistant, and hygiene appointments and regular check-ups are key to longevity.

First consultation from 250 PLN, standard implant with final crown 6000-8000 PLN (surgical + prosthetic procedures).
implantyOpole.whyHeading: Why choose a dental implant at Mikrostomart Opole
implantyOpole.whyIntro: Three elements determine the quality of implant treatment: the doctor's competence, technology, and materials. All three are at a premium level with us.
implantyOpole.why1Title: 🎓 M.Sc. RWTH Aachen + Oral Surgery Academy
implantyOpole.why1Desc: Dentist Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc. graduated with a Master of Science in Lasers in Dentistry from RWTH Aachen University (2021, with distinction — second in Poland, the youngest). Plus, Oral Surgery Academy — full surgical qualifications for advanced procedures (augmentation, sinus lift, All-on-4).
implantyOpole.why2Desc: Each implant is planned in 3D software (coDiagnostiX, 3Shape Implant Studio) based on CBCT and intraoral scan. The printed surgical template guides the drill with an accuracy of ±0.3 mm — minimal invasiveness, optimal position for the future crown.
implantyOpole.why3Title: 🧬 PRF from the patient's own blood
implantyOpole.why3Desc: Platelet-rich fibrin (PRF) from 10-20 ml of the patient's blood. Contains growth factors PDGF/VEGF/TGF-beta that accelerate healing and osseointegration. Standard in our implants since 2020 — at no additional charge.
implantyOpole.procedureIntro: Complete path of implant treatment: from the first consultation to crown placement.
implantyOpole.procedureStep1: Consultation + CBCT (250 PLN + 400 PLN). Medical history, clinical examination, cone beam computed tomography to assess the quantity and quality of bone. You receive a written treatment plan with price ranges.
implantyOpole.procedureStep2: Digital planning (1-2 weeks). We position the implant in 3D for the future crown. Surgical template printed in the lab. If needed — bone augmentation or sinus lift.
implantyOpole.procedureStep3: Implantation procedure (30-60 minutes). Anesthesia with The Wand, rubber dam, PRF from the patient's blood, titanium implant (NobelActive, Straumann, or equivalent), resorbable sutures. You return home independently.
implantyOpole.procedureStep4: Osseointegration (3-6 months). Titanium fuses with bone. We use LLLT (laser low-level light therapy) to accelerate healing. Temporary restoration available for visible teeth.
implantyOpole.procedureStep5: Final crown (1-2 visits). Intraoral scan with 3Shape Trios, CAD design, CNC milling 5-axis from a ceramic block (E.max press, translucent zirconia or Empress), try-in, cementation.

implantyOpole.faqHeading: Frequently asked questions about implants in Opole
implantyOpole.faqQ1: How much does a dental implant cost in Opole at Mikrostomart?
implantyOpole.faqA1: Standard implant with final crown 6000-8000 PLN — the package covers surgical procedures (implant placement under template control and ZEISS microscope, with PRF from the patient's blood) and prosthetic procedures (crown on the implant in porcelain or zirconia). The price includes digital planning, surgical template, and all follow-up visits during the first year. CBCT, bone augmentation, and potential sinus lift are priced individually after the first consultation. After the visit, you receive a written treatment plan with a detailed calculation.
implantyOpole.faqQ2: Can the implant in Opole be paid in installments?
implantyOpole.faqA2: Yes — larger implant procedures (All-on-4, full mouth) can be paid in 0% installments through Mediraty (up to 12 months) or internal installments with us (up to 6 months, no additional costs for regular patients). Details are discussed after the first visit along with the treatment plan.
implantyOpole.faqQ3: Does the National Health Fund reimburse dental implants?
implantyOpole.faqA3: No — dental implants are not reimbursed by the National Health Fund. The National Health Fund only covers partial and complete acrylic dentures (the cheapest solution aesthetically and functionally). All implants in Poland are a commercial service. However, German health insurance funds (gesetzliche und private Krankenversicherung) may cover part of the treatment costs abroad based on EU Directive 2011/24 — we issue detailed invoices in German.
implantyOpole.faqQ4: How long does the full implant treatment process take?
implantyOpole.faqA4: Standard schedule: consultation + CBCT (1 visit), digital planning (1-2 weeks), implantation procedure (1 visit, 30-60 minutes), healing and osseointegration (3-6 months), final crown (1-2 visits). Total 4-7 months. In selected cases (sufficient bone, single tooth), AnyTime Loading is possible (temporary crown immediately after implantation) — total 3 months.
implantyOpole.faqQ5: Does the implant hurt? What anesthesia do you use?
implantyOpole.faqA5: The implantation procedure itself is painless — we perform it under local anesthesia using The Wand system (computer-controlled, almost imperceptible). After the procedure, most of our patients stop taking painkillers after the first day (thanks to LLLT — laser low-level light therapy + PRF accelerates healing). For anxious patients, sedation with nitrous oxide ('laughing gas', 150 PLN) or pharmacological premedication (80 PLN) is available.
implantyOpole.faqQ6: How long does the implant last? Is there a warranty?
implantyOpole.faqA6: Titanium implants (NobelActive, Straumann, Astra Tech) — with proper hygiene and regular check-ups — last 25-30+ years. Ceramic crown (E.max, zirconia) — 15-20 years (depends on bruxism, material, hygiene). Manufacturer's warranty on titanium implant: lifetime. Our warranty on clinical work: 5 years on the implant + 2 years on the crown. Condition: regular hygiene visits every 6 months.
implantyOpole.ctaLead: First implant consultation from 250 PLN — you will find out if an implant is right for you, how much it will cost, and how long the entire process will take. No obligations.

leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.heroLead: The ZEISS Extaro microscope magnifies tooth canals 4-25 times. PIPS/SWEEPS lasers disinfect areas unreachable by instruments. Re-endodontics for teeth deemed lost in other clinics. Marcin Nowosielski M.Sc.'s specialty — Endodontic Curriculum PTE, member of ESE, author of 4 publications in the Dental Magazine.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.whyIntro: Endodontics is detective work. 30% of success is locating each canal (including the hidden MB2 in upper molars), 30% is disinfecting lateral canals, 30% is a tight seal. Without a microscope and lasers, all three stages are a compromise.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.why1Desc: 25× magnification allows seeing a canal with a diameter of 0.1 mm — inaccessible to the naked eye. 4 light modes (white, blue, contrast filters) reveal cracks, additional canals, and previous treatment errors. We locate MB2 (the second mesial-buccal canal of the upper molar) which is missed in 60% of standard treatments.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.why2Desc: Bubbles in an aqueous NaOCl + EDTA solution implode under the laser pulse, generating turbulent flow — disinfection penetrates lateral canals and dentinal tubules unreachable by instruments. Saydjari 2016: 99% elimination of Enterococcus faecalis at 1mm depth in the canals (vs. ~30% with manual NaOCl).
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.why3Desc: 70-85% success rate for teeth after an unsuccessful first root canal treatment (clinical literature). Typical case: patient after 5-10 years with pain, abscess, X-ray shows missed MB2 or underfilled canal. Requires removal of old gutta-percha, searching the entire canal system under a microscope, disinfection with PIPS/SWEEPS, and re-filling.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureHeading: Root canal treatment procedure step by step
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureIntro: Complete endodontic treatment protocol under a microscope — from diagnosis to crown placement.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureStep1: Diagnostics + CBCT (250 PLN + 400 PLN). Interview, pulp vitality test, periapical X-ray, sometimes CBCT for multi-canal teeth. Treatment plan with price ranges and prognosis.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureStep2: Anesthesia The Wand + rubber dam (15 minutes). Computer-controlled local anesthesia (almost imperceptible), rubber dam isolates the tooth from saliva — sterile working field. Patient breathes freely through the nose.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureStep3: Opening + locating canals under the microscope (30-60 minutes). Access drilling, locating each canal with ZEISS Extaro 25× zoom. For upper molars, we look for MB2 (the second mesial-buccal canal). Measuring working length with apex locator.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureStep4: Preparation + irrigation + SWEEPS (45-90 minutes). Reciproc R25/R40/R50 (rotating nickel-titanium files) prepare the canal. Irrigation with 5.25% NaOCl + 17% EDTA. SWEEPS Er:YAG laser (20mJ, 15Hz, 25µs) — disinfection of lateral canals and dentinal tubules. Drying with sterile paper points.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.procedureStep5: Filling + restoration (30 minutes). Warm gutta-percha + AH+ sealer (or MTA for open apices) fill the entire 3D canal system. Composite restoration with SonicFill. Control X-ray. Recommendation for final crown in 2-4 weeks (protects against fracture).

leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqHeading: Frequently asked questions about root canal treatment in Opole
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ1: How much does root canal treatment in Opole under a microscope cost?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA1: Standard: one canal from 800 PLN, 2-canal tooth from 1100 PLN, 3-4-canal tooth (molar, wisdom tooth) from 1500-1800 PLN. Re-endodontics (second treatment after an unsuccessful first) from 1500 PLN — requires removal of old filling and full revision of the canal system. The price includes CBCT if required, The Wand anesthesia, rubber dam, ZEISS microscope, PIPS/SWEEPS lasers, all irrigations, and control X-rays. After the first visit, you receive a written treatment plan with a detailed calculation.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ2: How painful is root canal treatment under a microscope?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA2: The procedure is painless — we perform it under local anesthesia using The Wand system (computer-controlled, almost imperceptible). Most patients discontinue pain medication after 24 hours (SWEEPS laser + tight filling reduce inflammatory response). Exception: acute inflammatory conditions (abscess, irreversible pulpitis) — the first 2-3 days may have sensitivity to biting, subsiding after starting antibiotics.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ3: How many visits will full root canal treatment take?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA3: Front teeth and premolars with straight canals: usually 1 visit (90 min). Molars with 3-4 canals: 1-2 visits (each 60-90 min). Re-endodontics: 2 visits with a 2-week break for calcium disinfection. After endodontics, we recommend a final crown within 2-4 weeks (protects against fracture) — this is an additional 1-2 visits.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ4: Why is treatment under a microscope more effective than without a microscope?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA4: Three reasons: (1) Locating MB2 — the second mesial-buccal canal in upper molars occurs in 60-80% of cases, but without a microscope, it is missed in 60% of treatments. Missed canal = chronic inflammation = extraction in 5-10 years. (2) Identifying cracks — microcracks in enamel/dentin visible only in 25× zoom determine whether a tooth can be saved or not. (3) Precision of preparation — preparing to working length ±0.5 mm instead of 'by feel' = better filling = fewer long-term complications.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ5: Does the National Health Fund reimburse root canal treatment under a microscope?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA5: The National Health Fund reimburses ONLY root canal treatment for front teeth (1-3) to a limited extent — without a microscope, without modern disinfection protocols. Endodontics for lateral teeth (4-8) and any endodontics under a microscope are commercial services. This applies to every clinic in Poland. German health insurance may cover part of the treatment costs abroad based on EU Directive 2011/24 — we issue invoices in German.
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqQ6: What guarantee do you provide for root canal treatment?
leczenieKanaloweOpoleMikroskop.faqA6: Endodontics in our clinic has control X-rays after 6 and 12 months (included in the price). If a problem requiring re-endodontics of THE SAME tooth arises within 12 months — we do it for free (with the final crown preserved and regular hygiene visits). The long-term effectiveness of our endodontics is 92-95% with 5-year observation (vs. 70-80% industry average) — the effect of combining microscope + lasers + ESE Quality Guidelines.
